records.title: ACCC issues draft determination to Google LLC and Epic Games, Inc.
records.summary: On 22 July 2026, the ACCC issued a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation for Google LLC and Epic Games, Inc to give effect to certain terms of a settlement the parties have reached to resolve ongoing litigation between them in Australia.

records.title: Dusk in court over sale of thousands of allegedly non-compliant button battery products
records.summary: The ACCC has commenced Federal Court proceedings against Dusk Australasia Pty Ltd (Dusk) (a wholly-owned subsidiary of Dusk Group Limited, ASX: DSK), alleging it supplied homewares containing button batteries that did not comply with mandatory button battery safety and information standards, in breach of the Australian Consumer Law.

records.title: Zoetis’ acquisition of Neogen Genomics requires Phase 2 review
records.summary: Zoetis Holdings LLC’s (Zoetis) proposed acquisition of Neogen Corporation’s global animal genomic testing business (Neogen Genomics) could substantially lessen competition and requires an in-depth Phase 2 assessment, the ACCC has decided.

records.title: Origin Energy to refund customers after ACCC investigation
records.summary: Origin Energy Limited will refund over $270,000 to more than 4,500 customers after an ACCC investigation into allegedly misleading representations regarding Origin’s “Ongoing Saver” residential electricity plan.

records.title: Non-bank lenders join Consumer Data Right as next stage commences
records.summary: The ACCC welcomes the commencement of new obligations today requiring non-bank lenders to begin sharing product data such as interest rates, fees, charges and eligibility criteria through the Consumer Data Right (CDR).

records.title: East coast gas market to be well supplied in Q4 2026, but more investment needed to meet long-term demand
records.summary: Wholesale gas on Australia’s east coast is expected to be well supplied in quarter 4 of 2026 (October to December 2026), but further investment is needed to meet long-term demand, the ACCC's latest Gas Inquiry report reveals.

records.title: Growing home battery uptake delivering lower electricity bills, but consumer protections needed
records.summary: Home battery uptake continues to surge with more than 400,000 batteries installed across Australia in past 12 months, but energy specific consumer protection reforms are needed to address a range of emerging issues, the ACCC’s latest Electricity Market Inquiry report reveals.

records.title: ACCC grants exemptions from the Cash Acceptance Industry Codes to motor fuel retailers Petro National Pty Ltd and Ampol Australia Petroleum Pty Ltd (50 sites)
records.summary: The ACCC has granted exemptions to Petro National Pty Ltd and Ampol Australia Petroleum Pty Ltd from the requirement to accept cash for certain in-person payments under the Competition and Consumer (Industry Codes—Cash Acceptance) Regulations 2025 (the Cash Acceptance Industry Codes).

records.title: ACCC statement on H5N1 response
records.summary: The ACCC acknowledges discussions held by Commonwealth and State Emergency Animal Disease officials over the past week about the threat of H5 bird flu in commercial poultry flocks.

records.title: JustAnswer to pay $10m in penalties for misleading pricing representations and misleading affiliation claims
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court has ordered JustAnswer LLC to pay penalties of $10 million for making misleading representations to consumers about the cost of its online question and answer service, and making misleading claims that it was approved or affiliated with the Fair Work Ombudsman when this was not the case, in proceedings brought by the ACCC.

records.title: New taskforce to tackle 'scambling' as fake online casinos target First Nations Australians
records.summary: Australians are being lured into fake online gambling and casino platforms that manipulate results, withhold ‘winnings’ and incentivise users into recruiting others, prompting the National Anti-Scam Centre to launch its latest cross-sector fusion cell targeting this emerging scam known as ‘scambling’.

records.title: New bans on water market manipulation and insider trading now in force
records.summary: New laws now require water markets decisions to be announced and ban market manipulation and insider trading in Murray-Darling Basin water markets.

records.title: Saipem Subsea7 merger requires Phase 2 review
records.summary: Saipem S.p.A’s proposed merger with Subsea7 S.A could substantially lessen competition and requires an in-depth Phase 2 assessment, the ACCC has decided.

records.title: Lactalis pays penalties over alleged misleading ‘fresh’ milk claims
records.summary: Lactalis Australia Pty Ltd has paid $59,400 in penalties after the ACCC issued it with three infringement notices for allegedly making false or misleading representations in the labelling of two of its milk products.

records.title: ACCC opposes Coles’ acquisition of a supermarket and liquor site in Kalgoorlie
records.summary: The ACCC has decided that Coles Supermarkets Australia Pty Ltd (ASX: COL) (Coles) must not put into effect a proposed acquisition of a leasehold interest over a new supermarket and liquor site in Kalgoorlie-Boulder, Western Australia.

records.title: ACCC welcomes new mobile coverage map standard and warns on misleading coverage claims
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Telecommunications and Internet
records.summary: The ACCC welcomes a new industry standard for mobile phone coverage maps, and urges mobile providers to ensure their network coverage claims are accurate and do not mislead consumers.

records.title: ACCC issues class exemption for global supply chain disruptions
records.summary: This short-term class exemption provides legal protection for competing businesses to respond to harm to Australian consumers or the Australian economy caused by global supply chains disruptions.

records.title: Amazon in court for introducing ads to Prime Video using allegedly unfair contract terms
records.summary: The ACCC has commenced Federal Court proceedings against Amazon Commercial Services Pty Ltd (Amazon AU) for allegedly breaching the Australian Consumer Law by including unfair contract terms in its Prime subscriptions contracts, and later relying on those terms to introduce advertising to its streaming service, Amazon Prime Video.

records.title: Trescal’s acquisition of TR Calibration requires Phase 2 review
records.summary: Trescal Holding Pty Ltd’s (Trescal) proposed acquisition of TR Pty Ltd’s calibration and equipment sales business, TechRentals Calibration & Sales (TR Calibration) could substantially lessen competition and requires further in-depth assessment by the ACCC.

records.title: ACCC to monitor fuel prices and market behaviour as fuel excise is partly restored
records.summary: The ACCC will continue to closely monitor fuel price changes following the Australian Government’s announcement that it will extend the temporary cut in fuel excise at a reduced rate.

records.title: Online travel agency eDreams pays penalties and gives undertaking over allegedly misleading subscription prices
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: Online travel agency Vacaciones eDreams, S.L, trading as eDreams, has paid $59,400 in penalties after the ACCC issued it with three infringement notices for allegedly making misleading pricing claims about its subscription-based memberships and failing to prominently display the total cost of an annual subscription.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation for preparatory steps to establish joint venture for commercial cash distribution
records.summary: On 26 June 2026, the ACCC granted interim authorisation to ANZ, CBA, NAB and Westpac to commence taking certain preparatory steps in relation to a proposed joint venture to deal with the supply of commercial cash in Australia.

records.title: ACCC to monitor supermarket compliance with new excessive pricing prohibition
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Pricing, Food and Groceries
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC will begin monitoring supermarket pricing from 1 July when the new excessive pricing prohibition comes into force.

records.title: Online retailer HSK United pays penalties for allegedly misleading pricing and returns policy
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Pricing, Digital Platforms and Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: HSK United Pty Ltd, which operates the websites Pain Free Aussies and Modern Aussies, has paid $79,200 in penalties after the ACCC issued it with four infringement notices for alleged breaches of the Australian Consumer Law.

records.title: ACCC grants authorisation for air cargo alliance
records.summary: On 25 June 2026, the ACCC granted authorisation to IAG Cargo Limited (IAGC), MAB Kargo Sdn.

records.title: ACCC proposes to re-authorise Job Futures Ltd (trading as CoAct) to collectively tender for social services contracts
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to re-authorise Job Futures Ltd (trading as CoAct) to collectively tender for social services contracts.

records.title: ARMA Group and Force Legal in Court over more than 320,000 allegedly misleading debt notices
records.summary: The ACCC is taking legal action against two related companies that between them sent more than 320,000 debt enforcement notices which were allegedly misleading and in breach of the Australian Consumer Law.

records.title: Cyclone reinsurance pool reduces premiums in high-risk areas but affordability pressures persist
records.summary: The Australian Government’s cyclone reinsurance pool is helping to reduce insurance premiums or moderate premium increases for people living in areas at higher risk of cyclones, the ACCC’s fifth and final insurance monitoring report has found.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation to Google and Epic
records.summary: On 23 June 2026, the ACCC granted interim authorisation to Google LLC and Epic Games.

records.title: Miyagi and its CEO in court over alleged unfair contract terms and misrepresentations in health program sales
records.summary: The ACCC has instituted proceedings in the Federal Court against Miyagi Pty Ltd, a company offering health and wellbeing programs, and its founder and CEO, Shane Da Costa.

records.title: Doreen Egg pays penalties after allegedly making false free-range egg claims
records.summary: Doreen Egg Aust Pty Ltd has paid $39,600 in penalties after the ACCC issued two infringement notices for alleged false or misleading claims that its eggs were free-range.

records.title: Online marketplaces pledge to do more to protect consumers from unsafe products
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Stay Protected, Digital Platforms and Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has raised the bar for online marketplaces to protect consumers from unsafe products bought online, strengthening the Australian Product Safety Pledge in collaboration with major online marketplaces.

records.title: ACCC decides not to authorise Screen Producers Australia to negotiate with broadcasters and streaming platforms
records.summary: On 18 June 2026, the ACCC published its decision not to grant authorisation to Screen Producers Australia and its producer members to collectively negotiate with television broadcasters and major streaming platforms to develop model terms of trade or to collectively resolve model terms amongst itself and its members.

records.title: Fitness support company pays penalty over 'If you have NDIS funding, it's covered' ad
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: WeFlex Pty Ltd, a fitness support company that is an NDIS provider, has paid a penalty of $19,800 after the ACCC issued it with an infringement notice for allegedly making a misleading statement about NDIS funding in a social media advertisement.

records.title: Households receive consistent broadband speeds on fixed-line and wireless plans
records.summary: Australian households with fixed-line (not wireless or satellite) NBN connections received on average 99.4 per cent of their internet plan’s download speed during the peak evening period when network demand is highest, the ACCC’s latest Measuring Broadband Australia report finds.

records.title: Joint Statement: Regulators strengthen joint oversight of digital platforms
records.summary: The Digital Platform Regulators Forum (DP-REG) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding to strengthen coordination in their regulation of digital platforms.

records.title: Airlines increase airfares and reduce flights on some routes as fuel costs rise
records.summary: Australia’s domestic airlines have responded to higher jet fuel prices by increasing airfares and reducing seat capacity on some routes, the ACCC’s latest Domestic Airline Competition report has found.

records.title: Grill'd in court for allegedly misleading customers about 'Tree Day Tuesday' donations
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has commenced proceedings in the Federal Court against Grill’d for allegedly making false or misleading representations to customers about the circumstances in which it would make donations to an environmental cause, in breach of the Australian Consumer Law.

records.title: Ugg boot retailer pays penalties for strikethrough pricing claims
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Ugg footwear retailer Christofi Investments has paid $39,600 in penalties after the ACCC issued it with two infringement notices for allegedly making misleading pricing claims on the Ugg Originals and Ugg Australia Classic websites.

records.title: Hismile pays penalties for false and misleading social media videos
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Hismile, an Australian oral and personal care company, has paid $138,600 in penalties after the ACCC issued it with seven infringement notices for alleged false and misleading representations in its social media advertising.

records.title: Shipping company Sea Swift amends contracts with remote customers following ACCC investigation
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions
records.summary: Northern Australian shipping and logistics company, Sea Swift Pty Ltd, will amend its transport contracts after action by the ACCC.

records.title: Express Online Training found to have misled consumers about RSA and White Card courses
records.summary: The Federal Court has found that training provider RSA Express (trading as Express Online Training) engaged in misleading or deceptive conduct and made false or misleading representations in relation to several of its online courses, in proceedings brought by the ACCC.

records.title: JB Hi-Fi to refund consumers after allegedly misleading “was/now” pricing
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: JB Hi-Fi Group has started a process to refund more than $250,000 to about 200 consumers after the ACCC raised concerns that the company may have misled consumers with pricing claims in advertisements for several products including two laptops, a VR headset and a gaming monitor.

records.title: ACCC grants authorisation for Australian Hotels Association members to engage in collective bargaining
records.summary: On 10 June 2026, the ACCC granted authorisation to the Australian Hotels Association, its associated entities and its members to engage in collective bargaining.

records.title: Thousands of scam websites taken down as online scams continue to cost Australians
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre is continuing its work to disrupt scammers as scams encountered online continue to drive the highest levels of loss.

records.title: ACCC calls for a stronger Horticulture Code as Fruitico and Fresh Express pay record penalties for alleged breaches
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Industry Codes, Agriculture
records.summary: Western Australian produce companies, Fruitico and Fresh Express, have each paid penalties of $99,000 after the ACCC issued each business with five infringement notices for alleged breaches of the Horticulture Code.

records.title: ACCC approves Ampol's acquisition of EG Australia, subject to conditions
records.summary: The ACCC has approved Ampol Retail Holding Pty Ltd’s (ASX: ALD) acquisition of EG Group Australia and EG AsiaPac Holdings (together, EG Australia), subject to conditions for Ampol to divest 41 retail fuel sites across Australia to an ACCC-approved purchaser.

records.title: ACCC issues takedown requests to Amazon, eBay, Kogan and Fruugo for toys and games containing potentially deadly small magnets
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is investigating the supply of banned and potentially deadly toys and games containing small high-powered magnets, including ‘magnetic chess’ or ‘magnetic battle chess’ games being sold tonline.

records.title: Peter Warren's acquisition of Wakeling Automotive sites requires Phase 2 review
records.summary: Peter Warren Automotive Holdings Limited’s (ASX: PWR) (Peter Warren) proposed acquisition of 30 new car dealership sites from Wakeling Automotive Group could substantially lessen competition and requires further in-depth assessment by the ACCC.

records.title: Amazon in court over alleged missing button battery warnings on children’s unicorn backpacks
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Digital Platforms and Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has today commenced Federal Court proceedings against Amazon Commercial Services Pty Ltd (Amazon AU) alleging kids backpacks in its possession or control for sale on its online marketplace failed to comply with mandatory button battery warning requirements.

records.title: ACCC proposes to grant exemption for the New Energy Tech Consumer Code
records.summary: ACCC proposes to grant exemption for the New Energy Tech Consumer Code

records.title: ACCC proposes to grant authorisation for air cargo alliance
records.summary: On 27 May 2026 the ACCC issued a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation to IAG Cargo Limited, MAB Kargo Sdn.

records.title: ACCC welcomes streamlined competition exemption powers to facilitate response to emergencies
records.summary: The ACCC welcomes new powers that allow for faster, streamlined exemptions to Australia’s competition laws in response to national emergencies and other exceptional circumstances.

records.title: Permanent ban on baby bottle self-feeding devices now in effect
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: A permanent ban on baby bottle self-feeding devices is now in effect.

records.title: ACCC consults on view not to oppose Australia Post stamp price increase
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Pricing, Postal Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is seeking feedback from businesses and consumers on its preliminary view not to object to Australia Post’s proposed stamp price increase of 8.8 per cent from mid-to late-2026.

records.title: Coles and Brownes pay infringement notice penalties for alleged Dairy Code breaches
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Industry Codes, Agriculture
records.summary: Coles Supermarkets Australia and Brownes Foods Operations have each paid $39,600 in penalties after the ACCC issued each company with two infringement notices for separate alleged contraventions of the Dairy Code of Conduct.

records.title: Court finds that Coles misled customers over 'Down Down' claims
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Food and Groceries
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court today found that Coles Supermarkets Australia made false or misleading representations about ‘Down Down’ discounts, in proceedings brought by the ACCC.

records.title: ACCC proposes to grant authorisation for Australian Hotels Association members to engage in collective bargaining
records.summary: ACCC proposes to grant authorisation for Australian Hotels Association members to engage in collective bargaining

records.title: ACCC welcomes additional funding
records.summary: The ACCC has welcomed the Australian Government allocating an additional $67.7 million over four years to further strengthen the ACCC’s competition and consumer law enforcement capabilities.

records.title: ACCC proposes not to authorise Screen Producers Australia to negotiate with broadcasters and streaming platforms
records.summary: ACCC proposes not to authorise Screen Producers Australia to negotiate with broadcasters and streaming platforms

records.title: Bedding supplier Emma Sleep to pay a total of $15m in penalties for misleading statements about sale prices
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court has ordered Emma Sleep Pty Ltd and Emma Sleep Southeast Asia Inc to pay a total of $15 million in penalties for making false or misleading representations about the sale price of mattresses, bed frames, pillows, and accessories.

records.title: Rising contacts raise concerns about electronics and whitegoods sector compliance with consumer guarantee rights
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Reports to the ACCC about consumer guarantees rose by 20 per cent to over 38,000 in 2025, compared to the previous year. About 70 per cent of people who contacted the ACCC in 2025 about an electronic product or whitegood raised issues relating to consumer guarantees.

records.title: ACCC granted leave to intervene in Epic v Apple proceedings
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions, Digital Platforms and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has been granted leave by the Federal Court to intervene in the Epic Games, Inc v Apple Inc proceedings, in relation to the relief to be ordered by the Court.

records.title: IAG's acquisition of RAC Insurance requires Phase 2 review
records.summary: Insurance Australia Group Limited’s (ASX: IAG) proposed acquisition of RAC Insurance (RACI) could substantially lessen competition and requires an in-depth Phase 2 assessment, the ACCC has decided.

records.title: ACCC authorises Countrywide Australasia to collectively bargain with suppliers
records.summary: ACCC authorises Countrywide Australasia to collectively bargain with suppliers

records.title: ACCC authorises the Uniting Church to collectively acquire insurance
records.summary: ACCC authorises the Uniting Church to collectively acquire insurance

records.title: ACCC investigating retailers making concerning Black Friday claims
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Pricing
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is investigating several retailers for making potentially misleading claims to consumers during last year’s Black Friday sales, following a sweep which found that around half of the 50 retailers reviewed made concerning claims in their advertising of Black Friday sales.

records.title: New merger control regime off to positive start
records.summary: Australia’s new merger control regime is working as expected and the ACCC is meeting its commitments on decision timelines, according to data from its first three months of operation.

records.title: ACCC decides MicroStar's acquisition of Konvoy requires Phase 2 review
records.summary: The ACCC has decided that MicroStar Logistics LLC’s (MicroStar) acquisition of the assets of Konvoy Holdings Pty Ltd (Receivers and Managers Appointed) (Konvoy) could substantially lessen competition in the market for keg pooling services and requires an in-depth Phase 2 review.

records.title: ACCC monitors fuel excise cut, fuel surcharges and fuel price movements
records.summary: The ACCC is warning fuel retailers they should fully pass on all fuel excise cuts by the Federal Government as quickly as possible.

records.title: NBN delivers steady performance, but some consumers missing out on full plan speeds
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Regulated Infrastructure, Telecommunications and Internet
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Most households are receiving internet speeds close to those promised in their NBN plans, however some problematic high-speed and fibre to the node services (FTTN) continue to fall short, the ACCC’s latest Measuring Broadband Australia report has found.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation to Qantas and American Airlines
records.summary: ACCC grants interim authorisation to Qantas and American Airlines

records.title: Storage vital to meeting winter demand across east coast gas market in Q3 2026
records.summary: Wholesale gas supply on Australia’s east coast is expected to be tight and large volumes of gas will likely be required from storage to meet demand in the third quarter of 2026, the ACCC’s latest gas inquiry report reveals.

records.title: Suppliers to ALDI, Coles, Woolworths and Metcash – new grocery supply agreement requirements start this week
records.summary: The revised Food and Grocery Code of Conduct is now in force.

records.title: Service quality and consumer protections to improve in proposed changes to NBN regulation
records.summary: Supporting more reliable internet services and ensuring investment in the NBN is prudent and efficient are key aspects of the ACCC’s draft determination on the regulatory settings for NBN Co to apply from 1 July 2026, released today.

records.title: ACCC updates the form and manner of the franchise disclosure register
records.summary: Following a consultation period with franchisors, the ACCC has decided to publish a new form which changes the way franchisors are required to provide information to the franchise disclosure register.

records.title: Continued action critical to combat fraud as annual scam losses exceed $2 billion
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre’s latest Targeting Scams Report highlights the complex, adaptive, and “wicked” nature of scams, and the critical role of collaboration in disrupting criminal networks, as Australians reported more than $2 billion in financial losses.

records.title: OPSM and Laubman & Pank franchisor pays penalty for alleged breach of Franchising Code
records.summary: Franchise operator Luxottica Franchising Australia which trades as eyewear retailers OPSM and Laubman & Pank, has paid a penalty of $19,800 after the ACCC issued it with an infringement notice.

records.title: Diesel prices rising faster than petrol, up 28 cpl across the largest cities in last week
records.summary: Retail petrol and diesel prices continued to climb in the week to 25 March as international refined petrol and diesel benchmark prices rose further, but diesel price increases at both the wholesale and retail levels are outpacing petrol prices, the ACCC’s weekly fuel price monitoring update shows.

records.title: Oil and gas services company Qteq and its chairman collectively fined $6 million for attempted cartel conduct
records.summary: Today the Federal Court has fined Qteq Pty Ltd and its executive chairman, Simon Ashton, $5 million and $1 million respectively for attempted cartel conduct, in a case brought by the ACCC. The penalty awarded against Mr Ashton is the highest penalty awarded for a competition law breach by an individual in Australia.

records.title: ACCC monitoring impact on domestic aviation amid Middle East conflict
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Regulated Infrastructure, Travel and Airports
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is closely monitoring Australia’s airline industry in response to unfolding events in the Middle East, a key development observed in its latest Domestic Airline Competition report.

records.title: PhotobookShop pays penalties for influencer reviews
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Online photobook business Tomsem Consolidated Pty Ltd, trading as PhotobookShop, has paid $39,600 in penalties after the ACCC issued it with two infringement notices for alleged misleading influencer reviews it posted on Instagram.

records.title: ACCC authorises fuel majors to coordinate to ensure fuel supplies, with conditions
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Petrol and Fuel
records.summary: The ACCC has granted an urgent interim authorisation to the Australian Institute of Petroleum, its members, and other relevant industry participants to coordinate in managing impacts to Australia’s fuel supply chain stemming from the conflict in the Middle East. The ACCC also published its second weekly petrol and diesel price monitoring update.

records.title: NDIS provider Aidacare admits to misleading customers about their consumer guarantee rights
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: NDIS provider of healthcare equipment, Aidacare, has admitted it likely made false or misleading representations to some customers about their consumer guarantee rights.

records.title: ACCC investigating diesel supply conduct in regional and rural Australia
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Petrol and Fuel
records.summary: The ACCC has launched an enforcement investigation into allegations of anti-competitive conduct by each of the major fuel suppliers, Ampol Ltd, BP Australia Pty Ltd, Mobil Oil Australia Pty Ltd, and Viva Energy Australia Pty Ltd.

records.title: Consumer impacts and regional supply issues at centre of discussions at emergency fuel meeting
records.summary: Motoring groups and the ACCC outlined to fuel companies the impact that rapid price spikes and fuel supply issues are having on consumers and businesses around Australia at an emergency meeting convened by the ACCC in Sydney and Melbourne today.

records.title: ACCC calling on industry to explain widely varying fuel prices
records.summary: Petrol and diesel price rises between 20 February and 11 March varied widely between Australian capital cities and in many cases have increased as fast as wholesale prices and in some cases by a greater extent, the ACCC’s new weekly fuel price monitoring update has shown.

records.title: Hyatt changes card surcharge practices as ACCC compliance monitoring continues
records.summary: Hyatt hotels in Australia have changed their card payment surcharging practices, following an ACCC investigation. After receiving reports from consumers, the ACCC investigated Hyatt Regency Sydney, and engaged with the regional group, Hyatt Hotels Corporation, about card payment surcharges.

records.title: ACCC to increase fuel market scrutiny and calls on fuel retailers to explain pricing
records.summary: The ACCC will urgently meet with fuel market participants to seek more detailed explanations for recent pricing conduct during the current Middle Eastern crisis, amid consumer concerns about sudden petrol and diesel price spikes and distribution issues in regional and rural Australia.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise the Uniting Church to collectively acquire insurance
records.summary: ACCC proposes to authorise the Uniting Church to collectively acquire insurance

records.title: ACCC keeping a close eye on petrol market amid Middle East conflict
records.summary: As international crude oil prices respond to the unfolding events in the Middle East, the ACCC is examining international and domestic fuel price movements and market behaviour.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise Countrywide Australasia
records.summary: ACCC proposes to authorise Countrywide Australasia

records.title: National Anti-Scam Centre taskforce report highlights value of joint effort to tackle romance scams
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre has released its latest fusion cell report today, detailing how a sector-wide crackdown on romance scams has helped to strengthen disruption efforts, enhance intelligence sharing and improve victim education and support.

records.title: Major airports increase infrastructure investment, but higher costs will likely flow through to passengers
records.summary: Infrastructure investment at Australia’s four largest airports increased by more than 43 per cent in 2024-25, however consumers could face higher airfares as airports seek to recover their costs by charging airlines more in the coming years, the ACCC’s latest Airport Monitoring Report shows.

records.title: ACCC authorises joint procurement of organics waste processing services for City of Newcastle and Port Stephens Council
records.summary: ACCC authorises joint procurement of organics waste processing services for City of Newcastle and Port Stephens Council

records.title: Drone supplier Tekron admits to resale price maintenance
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Pricing
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Agricultural drone supplier Drone TK Australia Pty Ltd (trading as Tekron) has admitted to engaging in resale price maintenance by directing certain resellers to not advertise or sell DJI drone products to consumers below a specified price.

records.title: Manipulative conduct in the digital economy, pricing claims and competition in essential services among ACCC priorities for year ahead
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions, Pricing, Digital Platforms and Services, Energy, Telecommunications and Internet
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC will renew its focus on manipulative and false practices in digital markets and the sale of dangerous goods online in the year ahead, and will also focus its compliance and enforcement efforts on addressing anti-competitive conduct in key sectors across the Australian economy.

records.title: Mobil to pay $16m in penalties for misleading statements about fuel sold at nine petrol stations
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Petrol and Fuel
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court has ordered Mobil Oil Australia to pay $16 million in penalties for making false or misleading representations about the fuel sold at nine petrol stations in north and central Queensland, in breach of the Australian Consumer Law.

records.title: ACCC seeks views on Australia Post's proposed stamp price increase
records.summary: The ACCC is seeking feedback from businesses and consumers on Australia Post’s draft proposal to increase stamp prices for ordinary small and large letters by 8.8 per cent from mid-2026.

records.title: Australians living with disability at risk of exploitation by NDIS providers breaching consumer laws
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Participants in the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) are being targeted by NDIS providers’ deceptive advertising practices and other behaviours banned by consumer law, a new report has found.

records.title: Understand how criminals exploit online relationships and inflict heartache
records.summary: In the lead-up to Valentine’s Day, the National Anti-Scam Centre has released its final quarter scams statistics, showing an increase in romance scams reports and losses across 2025.

records.title: ACCC authorises Sydney Water and a group of Sydney councils to procure the development of an organic waste bio-refinery
records.summary: The ACCC considers that the conduct is likely to result in a public benefit and that this public benefit would outweigh any likely public detriment.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise joint procurement of organics waste processing services for City of Newcastle and Port Stephens Council
records.summary: The ACCC is proposing to authorise City of Newcastle and Port Stephens Council a joint procurement of organics waste processing services.

records.title: ACCC decides Coles' acquisition of a supermarket and liquor site in Kalgoorlie, WA is subject to Phase 2 review
records.summary: The ACCC has decided that Coles Group Limited’s acquisition of a lease for a supermarket and liquor store in Kalgoorlie, WA requires an in-depth assessment.

records.title: Car rental platform pays penalty over concerning pricing representations
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: An international car rental booking platform has paid a penalty of $39,600 after the ACCC issued it with two ACCC infringement notices. SIA Booking Group Corporation, which operates under the name EconomyBookings, allegedly breached Australian Consumer Law in the way it displayed prices to consumers on its website.

records.title: ACCC refers Ampol's acquisition of EG Australia to Phase 2
records.summary: The ACCC has decided that Ampol Retail Holding Pty Ltd’s (ASX:ALD) acquisition of EG Group Australia and EG AsiaPac Holdings (together, EG Australia) requires further in-depth assessment by the ACCC.

records.title: WiseTech to divest Expedient following ACCC investigation
records.summary: The ACCC accepted a court-enforceable undertaking from WiseTech Global (ASX: WTC) and its subsidiary BluJay Solutions (Australia) Pty Ltd on 30 December 2025 to divest Expedient, a logistics software business, following an ACCC investigation.

records.title: The ACCC's holiday operation hours
records.summary: The ACCC wishes to inform you of our operations over the Christmas and New Year period.

records.title: Drone supplier EE Group admits to resale price maintenance
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Pricing
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Industrial drone supplier, EE Group Australia Pty Ltd (EE Group), has admitted to engaging in resale price maintenance by directing certain resellers to not advertise or sell DJI drone products to consumers below a specified price.

records.title: ACCC grants Port Terminal Access (Bulk Wheat) Code of Conduct exemption to CHSB Bulk Terminal at Geelong
records.summary: The ACCC has determined that CHSB Bulk Terminal Pty Ltd (CHSB) is an exempt service provider under the Port Terminal Access (Bulk Wheat) Code of Conduct (wheat port code) in relation to its port terminal facility at Geelong, Victoria.

records.title: ACCC urges households to change electricity plans to help offset price increases
records.summary: Households that have been on the same electricity plan for more than three years are paying on average $221 per year more than customers on new plans, the ACCC’s latest Electricity Market Inquiry report reveals.

records.title: City Beach ordered to pay $14 million in penalties for supplying non-compliant button battery products
records.summary: Fashion retailer Fewstone, trading as City Beach, has been ordered by the Federal Court to pay $14 million in penalties for selling non-compliant button battery products.

records.title: Tight supply forecast for Australia's east coast gas market in Q2 2026
records.summary: Overall gas supply on Australia’s east coast is expected to be sufficient in the second quarter of 2026; however, the southern states (Victoria, New South Wales, South Australia, Tasmania and the Australian Capital Territory) will collectively rely on surplus gas from Queensland and gas stores to meet demand.

records.title: Coles' lease acquisition in two Victorian locations not opposed subject to divestment
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Coles Supermarkets Australia Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Coles Group Limited (ASX: COL) acquiring leasehold interests to establish new supermarkets in Victoria at Mt Atkinson Major Town Centre and Deanside Central Town Centre, subject to an undertaking.

records.title: Keep this festive season stress free with scam safety checks
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre is urging Australians to check twice when shopping, gifting or travelling, as scammers know Australians will be caught up in the rush of the holiday season.

records.title: ACCC opposes Yamaha's proposed acquisition of Telwater
records.summary: The ACCC has decided to oppose Yamaha Motor Australia Pty Ltd’s proposed acquisition of Telwater Pty Ltd.

records.title: ACCC warns major retailers to not mislead or deceive consumers during Boxing Day sales
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has written to a number of major retailers ahead of the Boxing Day sales to remind them of their obligations under the Australian Consumer Law when advertising sales and promotions.

records.title: Wholesaler Golf Imports admits to engaging in resale price maintenance
records.summary: The ACCC has accepted a court enforceable undertaking from Golf Imports Pty Ltd after the golf equipment supplier admitted to engaging in resale price maintenance by prohibiting retailers from selling its products below a set price, in breach of Australian competition law.

records.title: ACCC allows collaboration for cash-in-transit initiatives and business continuity planning
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a final determination with conditions for the Australian Banking Association (ABA) and other industry participants to develop ways to ensure the continued distribution of cash across Australia, and to collaborate on business continuity measures.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise Sydney Water and Sydney Councils to procure the development of an organic waste biorefinery
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to authorise Sydney Water Corporation, Campbelltown City Council, Wingecarribee Shire Council and Wollondilly Shire Council

records.title: ACCC snapshot on AI developments highlights the need for continued monitoring of emerging technologies
records.summary: Products and services incorporating artificial intelligence (AI) technologies have continued to rapidly develop in 2025, with competition and consumer implications for Australian consumers and businesses, an ACCC industry snapshot on AI has found.

records.title: Equifax removes exclusivity in key contract with payroll and super data holder following ACCC investigation
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Banking and Finance
records.summary: The ACCC has accepted a court-enforceable undertaking from Equifax Australasia Workforce Solutions Pty Ltd in which Equifax has agreed not to enter into any new agreements preventing Equifax’s competitors from being able to access electronic payroll and superannuation data.

records.title: New high-speed NBN plans deliver faster internet speeds for households with FTTP and HFC connections
records.summary: Households that use high-speed NBN plans with a fibre to the premises (FTTP) or hybrid fibre coaxial (HFC) connection are now receiving faster internet speeds following the rollout of NBN Co’s recent upgrade program, the ACCC’s latest Measuring Broadband Australia report has found.

records.title: HelloFresh and Youfoodz in court over alleged subscription traps
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Food and Groceries
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has commenced separate proceedings in the Federal Court against home meal delivery providers Grocery Delivery E-Services Australia Pty Ltd (trading as HelloFresh) and Youfoodz Pty Ltd for allegedly misleading consumers over subscriptions.

records.title: ACCC proposes better protections for licensees and creators under music licensing arrangements
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to authorise the Australasian Performing Right Association’s (APRA) musical works licensing arrangements for five years, subject to stronger conditions aimed at improving the association’s accountability and transparency.

records.title: City Beach breached consumer law by supplying non-compliant button battery products
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Stay Protected
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court has found that fashion retailer Fewstone, trading as City Beach, breached the Australian Consumer Law by selling a range of consumer novelty products that did not comply with mandatory button battery safety and information standards.

records.title: Record high stevedoring prices despite significant spare capacity in ports
records.category: Rail, Shipping and Ports, Regulated Infrastructure
records.summary: Australia’s stevedores are charging record high prices and making historic profits despite having significant spare capacity in ports and with their costs and productivity remaining relatively stable in recent years, the ACCC’s Container Stevedoring Monitoring Report 2024-25 shows.

records.title: ACCC opposes IAG's proposed acquisition of RAC WA's insurance arm
records.summary: The ACCC has decided to oppose Insurance Australia Group Limited (ASX: IAG)’s proposed acquisition of RAC Insurance Pty Limited (RACI) from the Royal Automobile Club of Western Australia Inc.

records.title: Bupa ordered to pay $35 million for unconscionable conduct and making misleading representations about members' health insurance entitlements
records.summary: Bupa HI Pty Ltd (Bupa) has been ordered by the Federal Court to pay $35 million in penalties for engaging in unconscionable conduct and making false or misleading representations to Bupa members, hospitals and medical providers about the members’ entitlements to private health insurance benefits for certain claims.

records.title: Petrol prices higher across the largest cities in the September quarter but remained cheaper than 2024 levels
records.summary: Quarterly retail petrol prices across the five largest cities were higher in the September quarter 2025, according to the ACCC’s latest quarterly petrol monitoring report.

records.title: Commonwealth Bank pays penalties and offers redress for alleged breaches of Consumer Data Right Rules
records.summary: The Commonwealth Bank of Australia (CBA) has paid penalties totalling $792,000 after the ACCC issued it with four infringement notices for alleged breaches of the Consumer Data Right (CDR) Rules.

records.title: High demand for domestic air travel in spring led to higher airfares
records.summary: Very high demand for domestic air travel in September and October 2025 put additional pressure on seating capacity, and led to higher airfares for travellers in October, the ACCC’s latest Domestic Airline Competition report has found.

records.title: Commissioner appointments at the ACCC
records.summary: ACCC Chair Gina Cass-Gottlieb has welcomed the appointment of Dr Ian Oppermann as a Commissioner at the ACCC and the reappointment of Commissioner Anna Brakey for a 5-year term.

records.title: Igneo's proposed acquisition of Benedict Recycling not opposed following transaction restructure
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Igneo Infrastructure Partners' proposed acquisition of Benedict Recycling Pty Ltd, following a change to the transaction addressing the ACCC’s preliminary competition concerns.

records.title: Google ordered to pay $55m in penalties for anti-competitive conduct
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Digital Platforms and Services
records.summary: Google Asia Pacific has today been ordered by the Federal Court to pay $55 million in penalties for engaging in anti-competitive conduct when it reached understandings with Telstra and Optus about pre-installing Google Search on Android mobile phones.

records.title: ACCC statement on approach to determining the weighted average cost of capital
records.summary: The ACCC has today published a statement on our approach to determining the weighted average cost of capital (WACC).

records.title: ACCC begins inquiry into regulation of superfast fixed-line broadband networks
records.summary: The ACCC has today commenced an inquiry into whether the superfast broadband access service (SBAS) should continue to be regulated.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation to The Uniting Church in Australia National Assembly
records.summary: On 18 November 2025, the ACCC granted interim authorisation to UCANA to collectively negotiate, procure and maintain insurance and insurance-related products and services for the primary purpose of indemnifying and protecting the assets, revenues, activities and potential legal liabilities of UCANA, and a number of Synods.

records.title: Australians report nearly $260M in losses as shopping scams surge
records.summary: Australians reported nearly $260 million in losses to scams in the first nine months of 2025, with the National Anti-Scam Centre warning that online shopping scams are on the rise.

records.title: Farmers and families urged to stay safe on quad bikes
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Stay Protected
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has launched a quad bike safety awareness campaign in regional Australia warning that older male farmers remain the group most at risk of fatal injuries or crashes, with children also at risk.

records.title: Southern Cross Media's proposed acquisition of Seven West Media not opposed by ACCC
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose the proposed acquisition of Seven West Media Limited (ASX:SWM) by Southern Cross Media Limited (ASX:SXL).

records.title: Customers warned of recalled children’s sand due to asbestos risks
records.summary: The ACCC has published a recall notice over children’s sand products that may contain asbestos and were sold at various retailers including Officeworks.

records.title: John Holland ends agreements with CFMEU after ACCC investigation
records.summary: Construction company John Holland and the Construction, Forestry and Maritime Employees Union (CFMEU) have voluntarily terminated their agreements requiring John Holland to use only three specific labour hire businesses on two major infrastructure projects, following an ACCC investigation.

records.title: Soft plastic recycling scheme receives exemption from certain competition laws
records.summary: Soft Plastic Stewardship Australia (SPSA) and its industry members have been granted an eight-year authorisation by the ACCC allowing them to collect and recycle used soft plastic packaging under a voluntary scheme.

records.title: Independent petrol retailers give motorists choice and can lead to lower petrol prices
records.summary: Petrol prices are generally cheaper in cities that have more small-to-medium-sized independent petrol retailers competing with larger chains, the ACCC’s latest petrol industry report has found.

records.title: The Wiggles admit Emma Bow headband likely breached consumer law and commit to raise awareness
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Stay Protected
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Children’s entertainment group The Wiggles Holdings Pty Ltd (The Wiggles) has admitted it likely breached the Australian Consumer Law by selling Emma Bow headbands without mandatory safety warnings required for products containing button batteries, and has committed to raising awareness of the dangers that button batteries can pose.

records.title: ACCC puts retailers on notice ahead of Black Friday sweep
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC will conduct a Black Friday sales sweep to identify misleading or deceptive sales advertising used by retailers.

records.title: Most furniture suppliers fail mandatory toppling furniture safety rules
records.summary: Most furniture suppliers are failing to comply with new mandatory information rules that warn customers of the dangers of furniture tipping over, a sweep of businesses has revealed.

records.title: ACCC proposes to grant a Bulk Wheat Port Code exemption to CHSB Bulk Terminal at Geelong
records.summary: The ACCC has released a consultation paper proposing to grant CHSB Bulk Terminal Pty Ltd exempt service provider status under the Port Terminal Access (Bulk Wheat) Code of Conduct in relation to its port terminal facility at the Port of Geelong.

records.title: End-of-life battery disposal scheme allowed to continue
records.summary: The Battery Stewardship Council (BSC) has been granted an exemption for the next five years by the ACCC to allow it to continue to operate its B-cycle Battery Stewardship Scheme (Scheme).

records.title: Dash cam and car audio supplier Connected Audio Visual admits to resale price maintenance
records.summary: Connected Audio Visual Pty Ltd has admitted to engaging in resale price maintenance.

records.title: Microsoft in court for allegedly misleading millions of Australians over Microsoft 365 subscriptions
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has commenced proceedings in the Federal Court against Microsoft Australia and its US-based parent company Microsoft Corporation for allegedly misleading approximately 2.7 million Australian customers when communicating subscription options and price increases, after it integrated its AI assistant, Copilot, into Microsoft 365 plans.

records.title: ACCC proposes to allow continued collaboration to support Australians' access to cash
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to authorise the Australian Banking Association (ABA) and other industry participants to develop ways to ensure the continued distribution of cash across Australia.

records.title: Cuscal's proposed acquisition of Indue not opposed
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Cuscal Limited's (ASX:CCL) proposed acquisition of payment facilitation supplier Indue Limited after finding that the transaction is unlikely to substantially lessen competition.

records.title: ACCC opposes keg pooling supplier MicroStar's proposed acquisition of Konvoy assets
records.summary: The ACCC has decided to oppose MicroStar Logistics LLC’s (MicroStar) proposed acquisition of the assets of Konvoy Holdings Pty Ltd (Receivers and Managers Appointed) (Konvoy), both of which supply keg pooling services to brewers across Australia.

records.title: ACCC authorises the South Australian Chamber of Mines and Energy Inc's electricity buying group in South Australia
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a final determination granting authorisation to enable the South Australian Chamber of Mines and Energy Inc and participating members to form a joint electricity buying group, to pool their electricity demand, conduct a joint tender, and individually enter into electricity supply agreement with the chosen electricity supplier.

records.title: Elders' acquisition of Delta Agribusiness not opposed, subject to divestments
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Elders Limited's (ASX: ELD) proposed acquisition of Delta Agribusiness after accepting a court-enforceable undertaking that commits Elders to divest six Delta stores in Western Australia.

records.title: Luke Woodward appointed ACCC Commissioner
records.summary: ACCC Chair Gina Cass-Gottlieb has welcomed the appointment of Luke Woodward as a Commissioner for a 5-year term at the ACCC, commencing from 10 October 2025.

records.title: Yamaha's proposed acquisition of Telwater raises competition concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has outlined its preliminary competition concerns with Yamaha Motor Australia Pty Ltd’s proposed acquisition of Telwater Pty Ltd in a Statement of Issues published today.

records.title: ACCC authorises 1Circle to enable collective procurement of renewable energy supplies
records.summary: On 8 October 2025, the ACCC granted authorisation to 1Circle Pty Ltd, for it and the future members of 5 renewable energy buying groups, established over 5 years, to collectively procure and give effect to long term renewable electricity supply contracts which include an equivalent quantity of large-scale generation certificates.

records.title: Telstra fined $18 million for misleading Belong customers over broadband speed claims
records.summary: Telstra has been ordered by the Federal Court to pay a penalty of $18 million for breaching the Australian Consumer Law after it moved almost 9,000 Belong customers to a lower speed plan without telling them.

records.title: East coast gas supply outlook eases for first quarter 2026
records.summary: Gas supply on Australia’s east coast is expected to improve in the first quarter of 2026, but the outlook still depends on how much uncontracted gas LNG producers decide to export, according to the ACCC’s latest gas inquiry report released today.

records.title: ACCC authorises industry coordination to migrate card payments system to more secure encryption standard
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: On 25 September 2025, the ACCC issued a determination granting authorisation AA1000699 to enable Australian Payments Network Limited and current and future members of the Issuers and Acquirers Community to make and give effect to agreements and share information to facilitate migration to a more secure encryption standard.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise electricity buying group in South Australia
records.summary: The ACCC issued a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation to enable the South Australian Chamber of Mines and Energy Inc and participating members to engage in certain conduct related to forming a joint electricity buying group.

records.title: Digital platform regulators release working paper on immersive technologies
records.summary: The Digital Platform Regulators Forum (DP-REG) has published a working paper that examines immersive, or extended reality (XR), technologies and considers their impact on the regulatory roles of each DP-REG member.

records.title: Federal Court orders Optus to pay $100m penalty for unconscionable conduct
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Telecommunications and Internet
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Optus Mobile Pty Ltd (Optus) has today been ordered by the Federal Court to pay a penalty of $100 million for engaging in unconscionable conduct when selling mobile phones and contracts to hundreds of Australians, and subsequent debt collection in a case brought by the ACCC.

records.title: JustAnswer in Court for alleged misleading prices, subscription trap and government affiliation claims
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The ACCC has instituted proceedings in the Federal Court against JustAnswer LLC for allegedly misleading consumers as to its affiliation with Australian Ombudsman or Government agencies and the cost of its online question and answer service, in breach of consumer laws.

records.title: Final report for the inquiry to vary the superfast broadband access service access determination
records.summary: The ACCC has published its final report for the inquiry to vary the superfast broadband access service (SBAS) access determination, concluding the inquiry and outlining its findings and changes to the access determination.

records.title: ACCC authorises certification, suspension and termination provisions in Issuers and Acquirers Community Framework
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: The ACCC has revoked authorisation AA1000495 and substituted authorisation AA1000697.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation for certain conduct related to the New Energy Tech Consumer Code
records.summary: On 26 August 2025 the Clean Energy Council lodged an application for revocation and substitution, seeking authorisation for certain conduct relating to the New Energy Tech Consumer Code, including incorporating amendments to the code.

records.title: ACCC will not oppose acquisition of BGC Cementitious after changes to transaction
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose the acquisition of BGC Cementitious (BGC) by Cement Australia, Holcim, Heidelberg Materials Australia (HMA) and Adbri Pty Ltd.

records.title: Igneo's proposed acquisition of Benedict Recycling raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has outlined its preliminary competition concerns with Igneo Infrastructure Partners’ proposed acquisition of Benedict Recycling Pty Ltd in a Statement of Issues today.

records.title: Lower retail petrol prices in the June quarter reflect falls in international prices
records.summary: Retail petrol prices were lower in all capital cities and on average across regional locations in the June quarter 2025, according to the ACCC’s latest quarterly petrol monitoring report.

records.title: The Good Guys to pay $13.5m penalty for misleading store credit promotions
records.summary: The Federal Court has ordered The Good Guys Discount Warehouses (Australia) Pty Ltd to pay $13.5 million in penalties for misleading conduct in relation to several of its store credit and ‘StoreCash’ promotions, and for failing to provide store credit to more than 20,000 eligible consumers.

records.title: Fibre to the node trails all other NBN connection types for download speeds
records.summary: Households with a fibre to the node (FTTN) NBN connection are more likely to experience underperforming download speeds than any other fixed-line connection type, the ACCC’s latest Measuring Broadband Australia report has found.

records.title: IAG's proposed acquisition of WA's RAC insurance business raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has outlined its preliminary competition concerns with Insurance Australia Group Limited’s (ASX: IAG) proposed acquisition of RAC Insurance Pty Limited (RACI) from the Royal Automobile Club of Western Australia Inc in a Statement of Issues published today.

records.title: Four mobile crane companies in court over alleged cartel activity
records.summary: The ACCC has commenced civil cartel proceedings in the Federal Court against four mobile crane hire companies – Borger Crane Hire & Rigging Services Pty Ltd, MCR Melrose Pty Ltd, Two Way Cranes Pty Ltd and Ultra-Lift Cranes Pty Ltd – and four senior executives, for allegedly arranging not to supply services to certain customers or sites.

records.title: ACCC takes court action against fresh produce suppliers for alleged price fixing cartel
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Food and Groceries
records.summary: The ACCC has today commenced civil proceedings in the Federal Court against four suppliers and three senior executives for alleged price fixing while supplying fresh vegetables to ALDI, in breach of Australia’s cartel laws.

records.title: Court upholds record penalty in ACCC's cartel case against BlueScope
records.summary: The Full Federal Court has today dismissed appeals by BlueScope Steel Ltd and its former General Manager, Jason Ellis, against earlier Federal Court decisions in a civil cartel proceeding brought by the ACCC.

records.title: Full Court dismisses appeal by Delta Building Automation in attempted cartel case brought by the ACCC
records.summary: The Full Federal Court has today unanimously dismissed appeals brought by Delta Building Automation Pty Ltd (Delta) and its sole director, Mr Timothy Davis, against earlier findings that they attempted to rig a tender at the National Gallery of Australia .

records.title: Battery Stewardship Council's B-cycle scheme to continue under ACCC draft decision
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to grant an exemption for the next five years to allow the Battery Stewardship Council (BSC), to continue to operate its scheme to facilitate appropriate disposal of end-of-life batteries.

records.title: Owner of Noni B, Rivers, Katies brands ordered to pay penalties of $25m
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Former fashion retailer Mosaic Brands Limited has today been ordered by the Federal Court to pay $25.05 million in penalties for consumer law breaches that included accepting payment but failing to deliver items to consumers within a reasonable time.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise 1Circle to enable Business Renewables Buying Groups to collectively procure renewable electricity supply agreements
records.summary: On 27 August 2025, the ACCC issued a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation to 1Circle Pty Ltd for it and the future members of 5 renewable energy buying groups to collectively procure and give effect to long term renewable electricity supply contracts which include an equivalent quantity of large-scale generation certificates.

records.title: National Anti-Scam Centre calls for continued action this Scams Awareness Week as scam losses trend up at $174M
records.summary: Australians reported more than 108,000 scams and financial losses of about $174 million to the National Anti-Scam Centre’s Scamwatch service in the first half of 2025, with fake websites, online advertisements, and contact through social media criminals’ preferred methods of reaching people.

records.title: ACCC commences consultation on proposed National Broadband Network quality standards, entry level product and expenditure plans
records.summary: The ACCC is inviting submissions from users of the NBN, the telecommunications industry, and other interested parties on NBN quality standards and NBN Co’s expenditure plans and revenue requirements, among other matters to apply from 1 July 2026, in a consultation paper published today.

records.title: ACCC to investigate energy plans that potentially mislead consumers about savings
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC will investigate whether energy retailers are misleading consumers by promoting energy plans that promise savings or value to consumers when they actually offer poor value for the customer, following CHOICE making a designated complaint to the ACCC.

records.title: Google admits anti-competitive conduct involving Google Search in Australia
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Digital Platforms and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has today commenced Federal Court proceedings against Google Asia Pacific over anti-competitive understandings that Google admits it reached in the past with Telstra and Optus regarding the pre-installation of Google Search on Android mobile phones.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise industry coordination to migrate card payments system to new encryption standard
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to authorise coordination between Australian Payments Network Limited and members of the Issuers and Acquirers Community to migrate card payments system to new encryption standard.

records.title: MicroStar's proposed acquisition of Konvoy assets raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has outlined its preliminary competition concerns with MicroStar’s proposed acquisition of the assets of Konvoy, which is currently in administration and receivership, in a Statement of Issues today.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise certification, suspension and termination provisions relating to Issuers and Acquirers Community Framework
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to revoke authorisation AA1000495 and substitute authorisation AA1000697.

records.title: Victorian water corporations authorised to collectively acquire and generate carbon credits
records.summary: On 13 August 2025, the ACCC issued a determination granting authorisation AA1000696 to the Victorian Water Industry Association and 15 Victorian water corporations (the Participating Members) to enable them to collectively acquire and/or generate Australian Carbon Credit Units.

records.title: Domestic airline seating capacity continues to lag behind passenger demand
records.summary: The number of passengers flying domestically each month has returned to pre-pandemic levels.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise new scheme for soft plastics recycling
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation to establish a voluntary, industry-led scheme to collect and recycle soft plastic packaging from consumers.

records.title: Harvey Norman franchisor pays penalty for alleged breach of franchising code
records.summary: A Harvey Norman franchisor in Queensland, HN Macgregor Franchisor Pty Ltd, has paid a $15,650 penalty for allegedly breaching the Franchising Code of Conduct.

records.title: AusPayNet authorised to collaborate about the future of account-to-account payment infrastructure
records.summary: ACCC authorises Australian Payments Network Limited and Australian Payments Plus Limited for collaboration about the future of Australia’s account-to-account payment infrastructure.

records.title: Electricity industry on notice as more households invest in subsidised batteries and solar
records.summary: The ACCC is warning battery and solar suppliers and electricity retailers their sales practices must meet scrutiny as demand for home batteries and solar systems jumps due to subsidy schemes and the large savings that households on solar and battery plans are experiencing.

records.title: ACCC re-authorises the Australian Screen Directors Authorship Collecting Society
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a determination granting authorisation to the Australian Screen Directors Authorship Collecting Society Limited for 10 years.

records.title: Webjet to pay $9m in penalties for misleading statements about airfare prices and bookings
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement, Pricing, Travel and Airports
records.summary: The Federal Court has today ordered online travel agency Webjet to pay $9 million in penalties for making false or misleading statements about the price of flights and booking confirmations, after a case run by the ACCC.

records.title: Cyclone reinsurance pool lowering premiums in high risk areas but affordability concerns remain
records.summary: The Australian Government’s cyclone reinsurance pool has lowered insurance premiums for customers facing medium to high risk of cyclone, the ACCC’s fourth insurance monitoring report has found.

records.title: ACCC authorises collective bargaining and joint selling and advertising by Steeline members
records.summary: On 17 July 2025, the ACCC issued a final determination granting authorisation to Steeline Members Pty Ltd and its current and future members to engage in joint selling and advertising conduct and collectively bargain with suppliers.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation to Australian Payments Network Limited
records.summary: On 17 July 2025, the ACCC granted interim authorisation for AusPayNet to continue to give effect to the certification, suspension and termination provisions of the IAC Regulations and Code Set that were previously authorised while the ACCC considers the substantive application for reauthorisation.

records.title: Acciona's proposed acquisition of East Rockingham Waste to Energy Project raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has outlined its preliminary competition concerns with Acciona’s proposed acquisition of the East Rockingham Waste to Energy Project, which is currently in administration and receivership, in a Statement of Issues today.

records.title: Omnicom's proposed acquisition of Interpublic not opposed
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose the proposed acquisition of The Interpublic Group of Companies Inc by Omnicom Group Inc.

records.title: Power bank recalls on the rise due to serious burn and property damage risks
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Stay Protected
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is urging consumers to be alert to a growing list of recalled wireless power banks, which have the potential to cause serious burns and property damage.

records.title: ACCC grants conditional authorisation to collaboration between distribution networks
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a determination granting authorisation to Energy Networks Australia, Synergy, and other specified parties to allow for the procurement and implementation of a national public key infrastructure service.

records.title: ACCC authorises collaboration on sustainable finance initiatives
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a determination granting authorisation with conditions to allow the Australian Sustainable Finance Institute (ASFI) and industry participants to collaborate on sustainable finance initiatives for five years.

records.title: Lactalis' proposed acquisition of Fonterra's consumer, dairy ingredients and food service businesses not opposed
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Food and Groceries
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Lactalis BSA S.A.S (Lactalis)’s proposed acquisition of Fonterra Co-Operative Group (Fonterra)’s consumer, dairy ingredients and food service businesses.

records.title: Cleanaway's proposed acquisition of Contract Resources not opposed
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Cleanaway Waste Management Limited’s (ASX:CWY) proposed acquisition of Contract Resources Group Pty Ltd.

records.title: DP World Australia's proposed acquisition of Silk Logistics not opposed
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose DP World Australia Limited’s proposed acquisition of Silk Logistics Holdings Limited (ASX:SLH).

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise industry collaboration about future of Australia’s account-to-account payment infrastructure
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to authorise Australian Payments Network Limited and Australian Payments Plus Limited for collaboration about the future of Australia’s account-to-account payment infrastructure.

records.title: Consumers warned about 'ghost stores' imitating Australian businesses
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is warning consumers about the operators of four websites allegedly misrepresenting themselves as local businesses, also known as 'ghost stores'.

records.title: ACCC authorises Australian Payments Network Limited for conduct to wind down Australia’s cheques system

records.summary: The ACCC issued a final determination granting authorisation AA1000689 with a condition to enable Australian Payments Network Limited (AusPayNet) and members of the Australian Paper Clearing System to engage in conduct to facilitate the government’s wind down of Australia’s cheques system.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise Victorian water corporations collectively acquiring and generating Australian carbon credit units
records.summary: The ACCC has released a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation to the Victorian Water Industry Association (VicWater) and 15 Victorian water corporations to enable them to collectively acquire and/or generate Australian carbon credit units.

records.title: ACCC proposes to re-authorise the Australian Screen Directors Authorship Collecting Society
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation to the Australian Screen Directors Authorship Collecting Society for a period of 10 years.

records.title: ACCC grants Bulk Wheat Port Code exemption to Plum Grove Logistics at Geraldton
records.summary: The ACCC has determined that Plum Grove Logistics is an ‘exempt service provider’ of port terminal services provided via its port terminal facility at the Port of Geraldton in Western Australia.

records.summary: The ACCC has launched Federal Court proceedings against Edgewell Personal Care Australia Pty Ltd and its US-based parent company, Edgewell Personal Care Company or Edgewell PCC, for allegedly false or misleading claims that its popular Hawaiian Tropic and Banana Boat branded sunscreens were ‘reef friendly’.

records.title: Bupa in Court for unconscionable conduct and misleading consumers about health insurance benefits entitlements

records.title: Deteriorating short-term outlook for east coast gas supply

records.title: Digital Platform Regulators Forum

categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Unsafe products in online marketplaces will be a major focus of the ACCC’s product safety priorities during 2025-26, ACCC Chair Gina Cass-Gottlieb announced today.

records.title: Wireless networks provide high speed alternatives to remote and regional households
records.summary: NBN Co’s fixed wireless and Starlink’s satellite networks are providing Australians living outside of the fixed-line network footprint with high speed alternatives, according to the ACCC’s latest Measuring Broadband Australia report.
