records.title: Cash Converters and Mobile Travel Agents pay penalties for allegedly breaching Franchising Code of Conduct
records.summary: Franchisors Cash Converters Pty Ltd and MTA - Mobile Travel Agents Pty Ltd (MTA) have each paid a $16,500 penalty after the ACCC issued both companies with an infringement notice after they each allegedly breached the Franchising Code of Conduct.

categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services

records.title: Australia Post’s stamp price increase not opposed

records.title: Allianz’ proposed acquisition of RAA Insurance not opposed

records.title: More than 5000 Australian victims receive text warning over romance scam

records.title: No reward for being loyal: Australians urged to shop around for a better value electricity plan

records.title: The ACCC's holiday operation hours

records.title: ACCC accepts undertaking from TPG in ongoing investigation into Google's search services
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Digital Platforms and Services, Telecommunications and Internet
records.summary: The ACCC has accepted a court-enforceable undertaking from TPG as part of the ACCC’s ongoing competition investigation into Google’s search services in Australia, after recent undertakings accepted from Telstra and Optus.

records.title: High Court grants the ACCC special leave to appeal CFMEU/Hutchinson decision
records.summary: The High Court has granted the ACCC special leave to appeal a Full Federal Court decision handed down on 29 February 2024 which upheld appeals by the Construction, Forestry and Maritime Employees Union and construction company J Hutchinson against an earlier finding that they had reached an anti-competitive boycott arrangement or understanding.

records.title: Secure Parking to pay $10.95m for misleading consumers about Secure-a-Spot parking service
records.summary: The Federal Court has today ordered that Secure Parking Pty Ltd pay $10.95 million in penalties for making false or misleading claims about its pre-book “Secure-a-Spot” online parking service, following ACCC action.

records.title: Consumers warned of possible safety risks with the use of baby bottle self-feeding devices and children's car seat head straps
records.summary: The ACCC is investigating the safety of aftermarket children's car seat head straps and baby bottle self-feeding devices, after the Assistant Treasurer issued Safety Warning Notices for each of the products.

records.title: ACCC proposes to allow collaboration in the cash in transit industry
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation with conditions to the Australian Banking Association (ABA), its member banks, and other industry participants to facilitate business continuity planning in the cash in transit industry.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise price setting at shared dental practices
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation to the Australian Dental Association Inc and its members to continue to make and give effect to contracts, arrangements and understandings between 2 or more dental practitioners on the fees to be charged for dental services provided within a practice, where the parties operate in a ‘shared practice’.

records.title: Louis Dreyfus’ proposed acquisition of Namoi Cotton not opposed, subject to undertaking
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Louis Dreyfus’ proposed acquisition of Namoi Cotton Limited (ASX: NAM), after accepting a court-enforceable undertaking from the Louis Dreyfus Company B.V.

records.title: ACCC allows collective bargaining by retail, hospitality and accommodation businesses
records.summary: The ACCC does not object to the collective bargaining notification CB10000489 lodged by Delivery Angel.

records.title: Second National Anti-Scam Centre fusion cell to tackle job and employment scams
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre will coordinate a jobs and employment scam fusion cell to disrupt criminal groups advertising or offering jobs which do not exist.

records.title: Improved transparency lowers international money transfer costs, but switching promises extra $200m in savings per year
records.summary: While improved transparency has lowered costs for Australian consumers who send money overseas, consumers are still missing out on over $200 million in potential savings, a new ACCC report on international money transfers (IMTs) has found.

records.title: Dr Philip Williams appointed as ACCC Commissioner
records.summary: ACCC Chair Gina Cass-Gottlieb has welcomed the appointment of Dr Philip Williams AM as a Commissioner, proposed by Treasurer Dr Jim Chalmers in April.

records.title: Communique | Digital Platform Regulators Forum 2024
records.summary: Digital Platform Regulators Forum publishes yearly wrap up and strategic priorities to build capacity, promote regulatory coherence and respond to emerging risks and opportunities.

records.title: Final digital platforms report to focus on global developments and emerging competition and consumer issues
records.summary: The tenth and final report of the ACCC’s Digital Platform Services Inquiry will provide an update on recent developments in digital platform markets, identify emerging issues and chart major developments overseas and their impact on consumers and competition.

records.title: ACCC welcomes start of consultation on draft merger reform laws
records.summary: The ACCC has welcomed the start of consultation by the Government on draft legislation for proposed reforms to Australia’s merger laws.

records.title: DG Institute ordered to pay $14.7m in consumer refunds, and penalties of $5m, for misleading students in wealth seminars.
records.summary: The Federal Court has today ordered Master Wealth Control Pty Ltd (DG Institute) to pay $5 million in pecuniary penalties for making false or misleading representations to consumers in the promotion and sale of two education programs and to pay consumer redress totalling $14.7 million to students enrolled in one of the programs.

records.title: Consumers and businesses warned of potential scams following CrowdStrike outage
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre is warning consumers and small businesses to be wary of unsolicited calls, emails or messages requesting they download a software patch or provide remote access to fix or protect their computer from the CrowdStrike/Microsoft outage.

records.title: Investigation into APY Art Centre Collective claims discontinued
records.summary: The ACCC has completed its review of matters referred by the panel appointed by the South Australian Department of Premier and Cabinet concerning the operations of the APY Arts Centre Collective (APY ACC).

records.title: ACCC interim authorisation allows supermarkets to continue cooperation on soft plastics recycling
records.summary: The ACCC has granted interim authorisation with conditions to the major supermarkets Coles Group, Woolworths Group and ALDI Stores, to continue their collaboration to recycle stockpiled soft plastics and continue with the pilot in-store collection program.

records.title: Grays to redress consumers and pay $10 million in penalties for misleading descriptions of cars for sale by auction
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Cars and Vehicles
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court has ordered Grays eCommerce Group Limited, an Australia-wide online auction business, to pay $10 million in penalties, in proceedings brought by the ACCC.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise accreditation scheme by Victorian Electricity Distributors
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Energy, Regulated Infrastructure
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation to Victoria’s 5 Electricity Distribution Network Service Providers to enable them to continue to operate their scheme requiring civil contractors performing underground civil works involving electrical infrastructure assets to meet certain accreditation standards.

records.title: 'Oodie' pays more than $100,000 in penalties following alleged failure to put fire danger warning labels on Kids Beach Oodies
records.summary: Davie Clothing Pty Ltd, supplier of the popular loungewear brand ‘Oodie’, has paid $101,280 in penalties for allegedly failing to comply with a mandatory product safety standard.

records.title: Aurizon's proposed acquisition of Flinders Logistics raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has identified preliminary competition concerns with Aurizon’s (ASX:AZJ) proposed acquisition of Flinders Logistics Pty Ltd in a Statement of Issues published today.

records.title: The Good Guys in court over allegedly misleading store credit promotions
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has instituted proceedings in the Federal Court against The Good Guys Discount Warehouses (Australia) Pty Ltd, for allegedly making false or misleading representations about their store credit and ‘StoreCash’ promotions, and failing to provide store credit to eligible consumers, in breach of the Australian Consumer Law.

records.title: Criminals targeting victims of previous scams promising financial recovery
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre is urging Australians who have had money stolen by scammers to be wary of offers to recover their money for an upfront fee.

records.title: ACCC consulting on guide to sustainability collaborations
records.summary: The ACCC has today published a draft guide on sustainability collaborations and Australian competition law.

records.title: East coast gas supply sufficient for 2025 but concerns remain for longer-term
records.summary: Australia’s east coast gas market is forecast to have a surplus of between 69 and 110 petajoules in 2025 if Queensland’s LNG producers export all of their uncontracted gas, the ACCC’s latest gas inquiry report shows.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise collaboration in offshore energy feasibility licence processes
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Energy, Regulated Infrastructure
records.summary: The ACCC has released a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation to allow renewable energy businesses to collaborate to resolve overlapping geographic areas in their applications for feasibility licences under the Offshore Electricity Infrastructure Act 2021 and the Offshore Electricity Infrastructure Regulations 2022.

records.title: Stockland and Supalai's proposed acquisition of Lendlease residential masterplanned communities raises preliminary concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has published a Statement of Issues outlining preliminary competition concerns with Stockland and Supalai’s proposed acquisition of 12 Lendlease (ASX: LLC) residential masterplanned community projects through a joint venture.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation to ABA, banks and retailers to financially support Armaguard
records.summary: The ACCC has granted interim authorisation to allow the Australian Banking Association (ABA), banks and retailers to provide financial contributions to cash-in -transit business Armaguard.

records.title: ACCC accepts undertakings from Telstra and Optus during its ongoing investigation into Google's search services
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions, Digital Platforms and Services, Telecommunications and Internet
records.summary: The ACCC has accepted undertakings from Telstra and Optus as part of the ACCC’s ongoing competition investigation into Google’s search services in Australia.

records.title: Rain kept water availability high across the Murray-Darling Basin
records.summary: Wet conditions across the Murray-Darling Basin in 2022-23 kept water availability high, while regulated water charges varied substantially, reflecting the different tariff structures and approaches to cost recovery taken by each Basin State, the ACCC’s annual water monitoring report shows.

records.title: A milder winter and government rebates subdue the impact of increasing prices on electricity bills
records.summary: The ACCC’s latest electricity report shows that median effective prices for residential customers across all regions grew by 14 per cent between September 2022 and September 2023.

records.title: Dodo Power & Gas pays $82,500 in penalties for breaches of electricity code
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Industry Codes, Pricing, Energy
records.summary: Electricity retailer M2 Energy Pty Ltd, trading as Dodo Power & Gas (Dodo) paid $82,500 in penalties after the ACCC issued six infringement notices to Dodo for alleged contraventions of the Electricity Retail Code of Conduct (the Code).

records.title: Emerging technology risks among ACCC product safety priorities
records.summary: Risks associated with the use of emerging technology in consumer products such as lithium-ion battery powered products and virtual reality products will be a key focus of the ACCC’s product safety priorities

records.title: Fibre to the premises delivers most reliable broadband connection
records.summary: Australians that have a fibre to the premises broadband connection experience less outages than any other NBN connection type, the ACCC’s latest Measuring Broadband Australia report has found.

records.title: Regulation of the mobile terminating access service to continue following ACCC inquiry
records.summary: The ACCC has released its final report outlining the findings of its inquiry into whether the domestic mobile terminating access service should continue to be regulated.

records.title: Olam Agri Holding's proposed acquisition of Namoi Cotton raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has published a Statement of Issues outlining preliminary competition concerns with Olam Agri Holding’s proposed acquisition of Namoi Cotton Ltd (ASX: NAM).

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation to the Australasian Performing Right Association
records.summary: On 19 June 2024, the ACCC granted interim authorisation to the Australasian Performing Right Association Limited (APRA) to enable APRA to continue its arrangements for the acquisition and licensing of performing rights in musical works.

records.title: Novelty toy supplier and the operator of Timezone, Kingpin and Zone Bowling pay penalties for alleged button battery testing non-compliance
records.summary: Novelty toy supplier MDI International Pty Ltd (MDI) and the owner and operator of Timezone, Kingpin and Zone Bowling, TEEG Australia Pty Ltd (TEEG), have each paid $49,500 in penalties after the ACCC issued them with infringement notices for alleged breaches of the Australian Consumer Law.

records.title: Sigma and Chemist Warehouse's proposed merger raises competition concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has outlined preliminary competition concerns with Sigma Healthcare Limited’s (ASX: SIG) proposed acquisition of Chemist Warehouse Group Holdings in a Statement of Issues published today.

records.title: Average retail petrol prices were lower in the March quarter but reached a nominal high in April
records.summary: Retail petrol prices across Australia’s five largest cities were lower in the March quarter 2024, but daily average prices increased in April to a new nominal high, according to the ACCC’s latest quarterly petrol monitoring report.

records.title: ACCC proposes to reauthorise Tyre Product Stewardship Scheme
records.summary: The ACCC is proposing to grant authorisation for the Tyre Product Stewardship scheme to continue for a further 3 years.

records.title: ACCC authorisation for Virgin Australia to sell and market Air New Zealand’s trans-Tasman services
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: The ACCC has granted authorisation to allow unilateral code sharing arrangements between Virgin Australia and Air New Zealand that will boost travellers’ fare choices on trans-Tasman routes.

records.title: ACCC authorises joint procurement of waste services for Shellharbour City Council and Wollongong City Council
records.summary: The ACCC has released a final determination granting authorisation to Shellharbour City Council and Wollongong City Council to undertake joint procurement for waste collection services.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation for pricing at shared dental practices
records.summary: The ACCC granted interim authorisation to the ADA to continue to make and give effect to contracts, arrangements and understandings between 2 or more dental practitioners on the fees to be charged for dental services provided within a practice, where the parties operate in a ‘shared practice’ and at least one party is a member of ADA.

records.title: Icon Group's proposed lease acquisitions raise concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has published a Statement of Issues outlining its preliminary competition concerns with Icon Group’s proposed acquisitions of leases at: - St Andrew’s Ipswich Private Hospital in Queensland; - St Vincent’s Private Hospital Northside in Chermside, Queensland; and - St John of God Geelong Hospital in Victoria.

records.title: ACCC authorises Hunter Valley Coal Chain coordination for 10 years
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Rail, Shipping and Ports
records.summary: The ACCC has authorised Hunter Valley Coal Chain Coordinator Limited and coal chain participants to continue to engage in planning and coordination activities, including sharing information, for the cooperative operation and alignment of the Hunter Valley coal supply chain.

records.title: Delta Building Automation and its director to pay penalties for attempted bid rigging for National Gallery of Australia tender process
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions
records.summary: The Federal Court has ordered Delta Building Automation Pty Ltd (Delta) to pay a penalty of $1.5 million for attempting to rig a tender at the National Gallery of Australia, in proceedings brought by the ACCC.

records.title: Watch out for scammers claiming to fix technical issues, as losses spike
records.summary: Consumers are being warned to beware of anyone instructing them to download software or an app over the phone, following a recent rise in reported losses to remote access scams.

records.title: Industry to continue collaborating on cash transit arrangements
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: The ACCC has granted authorisation with conditions to the Australian Banking Association (ABA), the Customer Owned Banking Association, banks, retailers and other industry participants to allow them to continue developing responses to support the distribution of cash across Australia.

records.title: LG undertakes to take more steps to locate and fix dangerous solar batteries
records.summary: LG Energy Solution has provided an enforceable undertaking that it will increase its efforts to alert and protect consumers from faulty LG solar storage batteries which can overheat and catch fire without warning.

records.title: ACCC authorises water utilities to collectively negotiate for new gaseous chlorine supply
records.summary: On 13 December 2023, the Water Services Association of Australia applied on behalf of itself, and current and future WSAA Members who are water utilities that procure gaseous chlorine, for authorisation to engage in certain conduct for the purpose of improving the resilience of the supply chain of gaseous chlorine in Australia.

records.title: Global Payments' proposed acquisition of School Bytes raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has outlined preliminary competition concerns with Global Payments Australia 1 Pty Ltd’s proposed acquisition of School Bytes Learning Pty Ltd in a Statement of Issues published today.

records.title: ACCC authorises recruitment association’s professional conduct regime
records.summary: On 22 May 2024, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission granted authorisation to the Recruitment, Consulting and Staffing Association Limited.

records.title: Consumers lack visibility and choice over data collection practices
records.summary: The latest Digital Platform Services Inquiry report published today, highlights that consumers are generally unaware how much of their data is collected, used and shared with data firms and other businesses.

records.title: Return to pre-pandemic levels of airline travel and capacity
records.summary: Australia’s aviation sector appears to have recovered from the COVID-19 pandemic as passenger levels and capacity returned to pre-pandemic levels, the ACCC’s latest Domestic Airline Competition in Australia report reveals.

records.title: Two 4WD accessory manufacturers admit to resale price maintenance
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Four-wheel drive accessory manufacturers, MSA 4x4 Accessories Pty Ltd (MSA) and Offroad Animal Pty Ltd (Offroad Animal), have each separately admitted to engaging in resale price maintenance by instructing resellers to not advertise or sell their products below a specified price.

records.title: Louis Dreyfus Company's proposed acquisition of Namoi Cotton raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has published a Statement of Issues outlining preliminary competition concerns with Louis Dreyfus Company’s (LDC) proposed acquisition of Namoi Cotton Ltd (ASX: NAM).

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise joint procurement of waste collection services for Shellharbour City Council and Wollongong City Council
records.summary: The ACCC has released a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation to Shellharbour City Council and Wollongong City Council (the Applicants) to undertake joint procurement for waste collection services.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation to Victoria’s Electricity Distribution Network Service Providers
records.summary: The ACCC has granted interim authorisation to Victoria’s 5 Electricity Distribution Network Service Providers - AusNet Electricity Services, Citipower, Jemena Electricity Networks (Vic), Powercor Australia and United Energy Distribution.

records.title: New laws to increase awareness of the dangers of toppling furniture
records.summary: Furniture suppliers will be required to provide safety warnings to consumers about the dangers of toppling furniture hazards, after the Assistant Treasurer, the Hon.

records.title: Qantas agrees to $20m payments to customers and, subject to court approval, a $100m penalty for misleading consumers
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: Qantas has admitted that it misled consumers by advertising tickets for tens of thousands of flights it had already decided to cancel, and by cancelling thousands more flights without promptly telling ticketholders of its decision.

records.title: ACCC proposes authorisation for Virgin Australia to sell and market Air New Zealand’s trans-Tasman services
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation to Virgin Australia and Air New Zealand for proposed unilateral code sharing arrangements on trans-Tasman routes.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise Hunter Valley coal chain coordination and centralised planning activities for a further 10 years
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation to enable Hunter Valley Coal Chain Coordinator Limited (HVCCC) and coal chain participants to continue to engage in planning and coordination activities, including sharing information, for the cooperative operation and alignment of the Hunter Valley coal supply chain.

records.title: Rebound in passenger numbers helps major airports' financial recovery
records.summary: The aeronautical operations of Brisbane, Melbourne, Perth and Sydney airports returned to profit in 2022-23, the first full financial year since the end of COVID-19 travel restrictions, the ACCC’s latest Airport Monitoring Report shows.

records.title: Scam losses decline, but more work to do as Australians lose $2.7 billion
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre’s collaborative efforts across government, law enforcement, consumer organisations and industry have boosted the community’s fight against financial crime, as the latest Targeting Scams report reveals a 13.1 per cent decline in reported losses to $2.74 billion in 2023.

records.title: Metcash's proposed acquisition of Superior Food Services not opposed
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Metcash Trading Limited’s (Metcash) (ASX:MTS) proposed acquisition of wholesale food distributor SFG Group Holdings Pty Ltd, trading as Superior Food Services (Superior).

records.title: ACCC allows collective bargaining by small Australian mutuals
records.summary: The ACCC does not object to the collective bargaining notification CB10000486 lodged by The Customer Owned Banking Association (COBA).

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise recruitment association’s professional conduct regime
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation to the Recruitment, Consulting and Staffing Association Limited (RCSA) to continue to operate parts of its Professional Conduct Regime which sets out the professional standards for, and regulates the conduct of, its members in relation to the provision of recruitment and human resources services.

records.title: GLAD bags manufacturer in court for '50% ocean plastic' claims
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The ACCC has instituted proceedings in the Federal Court against Clorox Australia Pty Ltd, the manufacturer of GLAD-branded kitchen and garbage bags, for allegedly making false or misleading representations that certain kitchen and garbage bags were partly made of recycled ‘ocean plastic’, in breach of the Australian Consumer Law.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise collective negotiation by the Water Services Association of Australia and its members
records.summary: ACCC proposes to authorise collective negotiation by the Water Services Association of Australia and its members

records.title: ACCC grants authorisation for banks to establish a program to jointly procure assurance reviews of mortgage aggregators
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: On 11 April 2024, the ACCC granted authorisation with conditions for the applicants and other industry participants to develop and implement a program to collectively acquire assurance services in relation to mortgage aggregators.

records.title: ACCC welcomes proposal for stronger merger laws
records.summary: The ACCC welcomes the Government’s announcement today that it will move to strengthen Australia’s merger laws, bringing Australia into line with most other developed economies.

records.title: Dominique Grubisa and DG Institute made misleading representations to students in wealth seminars
records.summary: The Federal Court has today found that Master Wealth Control Pty Ltd, trading as DG Institute breached the Australian Consumer Law, and that its sole Director Dominque Grubisa was knowingly concerned in the contraventions, in proceedings brought by the ACCC.

records.title: East coast gas surplus expected in third quarter 2024
records.summary: Australia’s east coast gas market is expected to have a six petajoule (PJ) surplus in the third quarter of 2024, even if the three LNG producers export all their uncontracted gas, the ACCC’s March 2024 interim gas inquiry report shows.

records.title: ACCC will not oppose revised Cochlear, Oticon Medical transaction
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Cochlear Limited’s (ASX:COH) proposed acquisition of the cochlear implants business of Oticon Medical A/S from its parent company, Demant A/S.

records.title: Beware of fake invoices from scammers impersonating businesses
records.summary: Australians are being urged to check payment details directly with a business before paying an emailed invoice, following a rise in losses to payment redirection scams.

records.title: ACCC seeks leave from High Court to appeal CFMEU and Hutchinson boycott judgment
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions
records.summary: The ACCC is seeking special leave to appeal to the High Court from the Full Federal Court’s judgment that upheld appeals by the Construction, Forestry and Maritime Employees Union (CFMEU) and construction company J Hutchinson Pty Ltd from an earlier decision finding they had reached an anti-competitive arrangement or understanding.

records.title: HSBC pays penalties for alleged breaches of Consumer Data Right rules
records.category: Consumer Data Right, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: HSBC Bank Australia Limited (HSBC) has paid penalties totalling $33,000 after the ACCC issued it with two infringement notices for alleged contraventions of the Consumer Data Right (CDR) rules.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation to the Australian Banking Association
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: The ACCC has granted interim authorisation with conditions to the Australian Banking Association (ABA), its member banks, and other relevant industry participants to facilitate implementation of business continuity measures in the event of suspension or disruption to cash services in Australia.

records.title: Higher grocery prices force many consumers to make sacrifices, ACCC hears
records.summary: Many younger Australians and lower income households are spending up to one-quarter of their net income on groceries, according to responses so far to the consumer survey which is part of the ACCC’s Supermarkets Inquiry.

records.title: ACCC authorises joint renewable energy purchasing group
records.summary: On 27 March 2024, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) granted authorisation to 1Circle Pty Ltd and members of the Business Renewables Buying Group (the Group) to enable them to form a joint renewable energy buying group.

records.title: Internet performance in busy hours improves under new NBN pricing
records.summary: Broadband speed performance in the peak-demand period of 7-11pm (busy hours) has improved significantly for users of NBN ‘Home Ultrafast’ 1000 Mbps services, following the removal of capacity-based charging under NBN Co’s new wholesale pricing structure.

records.title: Regulation of wholesale telecommunications to change following ACCC inquiry
records.summary: The ACCC has released its final report outlining the findings of an inquiry into whether nine wholesale telecommunications services should continue to be regulated.

records.title: GrainCorp's proposed acquisition of XF Australia not opposed
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose the acquisition of animal feed supplement supplier, XF Australia Limited by Graincorp Limited.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation to facilitate offshore energy feasibility application processes
records.summary: The ACCC has granted urgent interim authorisation to allow renewable energy businesses to collaborate to resolve overlapping geographic areas in their applications for feasibility licences for the Gippsland and Hunter declared offshore electricity generation regions.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise Australian banks and other parties to discuss and develop industry solutions to support the cash-in-transit industry
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation with conditions until 31 October 2024 to the Australian Banking Association, its member banks, and other industry participants.

records.title: Retail petrol prices remain relatively high despite falling in the December quarter 2023
records.summary: Retail petrol prices in Australia’s five largest cities trended downward in the December quarter 2023 as international refined petrol prices decreased, according to the ACCC’s latest quarterly petrol monitoring report.

records.title: ACCC to examine internet search
records.summary: The impact of overseas reforms and technological developments, as well as trends in search quality, are among the issues to be considered by the ACCC as it considers the state of competition in general internet search services in Australia.

records.title: ACCC will not object to Australia Post price increase
records.summary: The ACCC does not object to Australia Post’s proposed price increase for its reserved ordinary letter service.

records.title: Online florist ordered to pay $1 million for misleading online star ratings and price representations
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Pricing
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court has today ordered Bloomex to pay $1 million in penalties after the online florist and gift retailer admitted that it had breached the Australian Consumer Law by making false and misleading representations on its website.

records.title: ACCC denies authorisation to Bakers Delight to implement price tiers in new point-of-sale system
records.summary: On 14 March 2024, the ACCC released a final determination denying authorisation to Bakers Delight Holdings Ltd to implement a price tiering system for a range of promotional products in its new point-of-sale system.

records.title: Aussie Broadband leads wholesale growth on the NBN
records.summary: Smaller broadband providers, Aussie Broadband, Vocus and Superloop, gained over 62,000 NBN wholesale services in the December 2023 quarter as the total number of residential broadband services on the NBN remained steady, the ACCC’s latest NBN Wholesale Market Indicators Report has found.

records.title: ACCC extends wholesale price controls to superfast fixed-line broadband networks
records.summary: Australians on broadband plans provided by non-NBN fixed-line networks should benefit from more stable pricing and greater competition between retailers, following the ACCC’s decision to make a final wholesale access determination for the declared superfast broadband access service (SBAS).

records.title: Viet Nam Competition Commission signs new Memorandum of Understanding with ACCC
records.summary: The ACCC and its Vietnamese counterpart, the Viet Nam Competition Commission (VCC), have committed to even closer collaboration in securing strong regulatory and economic outcomes by signing a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU).

records.title: Cost of living and digital economy shape 2024-25 compliance and enforcement priorities
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Energy, Food and Groceries
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Consumer and competition issues in the supermarket sector and essential services including electricity and financial services are among the ACCC’s compliance and enforcement priorities for the year ahead, ACCC Chair Gina Cass-Gottlieb announced today.

records.title: ACCC update on Tribunal’s decision to authorise ANZ's acquisition of Suncorp
records.summary: On 20 February 2024, the Australian Competition Tribunal set aside the ACCC’s earlier decision on 4 August 2023 not to grant authorisation for ANZ to acquire Suncorp’s banking business.

records.title: Noni B, Rivers, Katies owner Mosaic Brands in court for allegedly failing to meet advertised delivery times
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC today commenced proceedings in the Federal Court, alleging that national fashion retailer Mosaic Brands Limited breached the Australian Consumer Law by failing to deliver several hundred thousand products to customers within the delivery timeframes advertised on its websites.

records.title: CFMEU and Hutchinson appeals against boycott finding upheld
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions
records.summary: The Full Federal Court has upheld appeals by the Construction, Forestry and Maritime Employees Union (CFMEU) and construction company J Hutchinson Pty Ltd against an earlier judgment in favour of the ACCC.

records.title: It's a scam! Celebrities are not getting rich from online investment trading platforms
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre is warning consumers to beware of fake news articles and deepfake videos of public figures that endorse and link to online investment trading platform scams, particularly on social media.

records.title: Ultra Tune fined $1.5 million for contempt of court
records.summary: The Federal Court of Australia has fined Ultra Tune Australia Pty Ltd (Ultra Tune) $1.5 million for contempt of court.

records.title: ACCC and Philippine Competition Commission sign new Memorandum of Understanding
records.summary: A new Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), signed by the ACCC and its Filipino counterpart, the Philippine Competition Commission (PCC), will continue to strengthen cooperation between the two competition regulators.

records.title: ACCC supermarkets inquiry invites consumer, farmer and industry views
records.summary: Australian consumers are asked to share information about how they shop and what they experience through an online survey published today as part of the ACCC’s supermarkets inquiry.

records.title: Westpac's proposed acquisition of HealthPoint not opposed
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Westpac Banking Corporation’s proposed acquisition of HealthPoint.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise joint renewable energy purchasing group
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation to 1Circle Pty Ltd and members of the Business Renewables Buying Group

records.title: Review of past merger decisions reveals mixed outcomes
records.summary: The ACCC has released findings from reviews of three past merger decisions by the ACCC that were completed in 2019 in a report released today.

records.title: Grays in court over misleading descriptions of cars for sale online
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement, Cars and Vehicles
records.summary: The ACCC has instituted legal proceedings in the Federal Court against Grays eCommerce Group Limited, an Australia-wide online auction business, over false or misleading descriptions of hundreds of cars on its site, including about the make, model, features or undisclosed obvious faults with the cars.

records.title: Total Food Network pays penalties for alleged Horticulture Code breaches
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Industry Codes, Agriculture
records.summary: APMS NQ Pty Ltd trading as the Total Food Network Australia (Total Food) has paid $27,500 in penalties after the ACCC issued it with two infringement notices for alleged contraventions of the Horticulture Code.

records.title: Criminal sentences imposed on Bingo, Aussie Skips and their former CEOs Daniel Tartak and Emmanuel Roussakis for skip bin and waste processing cartel
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions
records.summary: The Federal Court has today convicted and sentenced waste management companies Bingo Industries, and Aussie Skips Bin Services and Aussie Skips Recycling (Aussie Skips) for criminal cartel offences under sections 45AF and 45AG of the Competition and Consumer Act relating to a price fixing arrangement for demolition waste services in Sydney.

records.title: Monash IVF's proposed acquisition of Fertility North not opposed
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Monash IVF Group Limited’s (ASX: MVF) proposed acquisition of Fertility North Holdings Pty Ltd.

records.title: Australian Competition Tribunal authorises ANZ’s proposed acquisition of Suncorp Bank
records.summary: The ACCC notes the Australian Competition Tribunal’s decision today to grant authorisation for ANZ’s proposed acquisition of Suncorp’s banking business.

records.title: Designated complaints legislation welcomed
records.summary: The ACCC has welcomed today’s introduction of legislation into Parliament that will enable certain consumer and small business groups to make designated complaints to the ACCC about significant and systemic market issues that relate to the ACCC’s powers or functions under the Competition and Consumer Act and the Australian Consumer Law.

records.title: realestate.com.au's proposed acquisition of Dynamic Methods raises potential concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has outlined preliminary competition concerns with realestate.com.au’s proposed acquisition of Dynamic Methods in a statement of issues published today.

records.title: ACCC authorises AMA NSW to collectively bargain with NSW Health and public health organisations
records.summary: On 14 February 2024, the ACCC decided to revoke authorisation A91383 and grant authorisation AA1000652 in substitution to enable the Australian Medical Association (NSW) Limited to continue to collectively bargain on behalf of visiting medical officers engaged in the NSW public hospital system, with NSW Health and public health organisations.

records.title: Mazda to pay $11.5m for misleading consumers about consumer guarantee rights for serious vehicle faults
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Cars and Vehicles
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court has ordered Mazda Australia Pty Ltd pay $11.5 million in penalties for engaging in misleading and deceptive conduct and making false or misleading representations to nine consumers about their consumer guarantee rights.

records.title: Domestic airfares were generally lower in 2023 but reliability remains poor
records.summary: Domestic airfares generally fell last year after reaching record highs in late-2022, but cancellations and delays remain higher than long-term industry averages, the ACCC’s latest Domestic Airline Competition in Australia report reveals.

records.title: Avoid heartache this Valentine's Day - never take investment advice from an online love interest
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre is warning people looking for love online to beware of financial criminals luring them into investment scams.

records.title: Proposed compulsory recall of dangerous LG solar storage batteries
records.summary: Consumers are being urged to check their solar energy storage systems for unsafe LG solar batteries, after the Assistant Treasurer today issued a proposed recall notice for specified LG solar storage batteries, which can overheat and catch fire without warning.

records.title: Evidence backs case for critical merger law reform
records.summary: Reform of laws governing mergers and acquisitions is urgently needed to bring Australia in line with other developed economies and ensure the merger laws are effective in preventing anti-competitive transactions, particularly during the current cost-of-living crisis, the ACCC has told the Treasury Competition Review.

records.title: ACCC authorises collective tendering for Southern Sydney Regional Organisation of Councils
records.summary: The ACCC has authorised Southern Sydney Regional Organisation of Councils its member councils to collectively invite tenders, evaluate responses and negotiate contracts for the processing of commingled recycling from yellow-lidded kerbside bins and disposal of residuals.

records.title: ACCC varies Australian Banking Association authorisation
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance
records.summary: On 1 February 2024 the ACCC issued a determination granting a minor variation to the Australian Banking Association’s authorisation to allow changes to certain minimum requirements of basic bank accounts offered to low-income customers under the Banking Code.

records.title: Changes proposed to make childcare affordable and accessible for all families
records.summary: After a year-long inquiry, the ACCC has found that childcare markets under the current regulatory settings are not delivering on key policy objectives for all households and communities, including in relation to accessibility and affordability.

records.title: ACCC consults on Australia Post’s proposed stamp price increase
records.summary: The ACCC is seeking stakeholder feedback on its preliminary view that it will not object to Australia Post’s proposal to increase the price of its reserved ordinary letter service by 25 per cent.

records.title: ACCC to examine prices and competition in supermarket sector
records.summary: The ACCC welcomes today’s announcement by the Australian Government that it will direct the ACCC to conduct an inquiry into Australia’s supermarket sector, including the pricing practices of the supermarkets and the relationship between wholesale, including farmgate, and retail prices.

records.title: Swifties beware: scammers are in their Cruel Summer Era
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Stay Protected, Scams
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Taylor Swift fans looking to secure last minute tickets to the Australian leg of The Eras Tour are being urged to be cautious of scammers selling fake tickets on social media.

records.title: Looking to earn extra cash? Don’t lose money to a side hustle scam
records.summary: Australians looking to earn extra money through online ‘side hustles’ are being urged to beware of scammers, after reported losses to Scamwatch for jobs and employment scams almost tripled in 2023.

records.title: Broken but out of warranty? Your consumer guarantee rights may still apply
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is encouraging Australians to take advantage of their consumer guarantee rights under the Australian Consumer Law when dealing with businesses over defective products or poorly performed services, following more than 28,000 reports and enquiries to the ACCC last year about consumer guarantees or warranties.

records.title: Web hosting business pays penalties for allegedly misleading customers about ‘free gifts’
records.summary: The owner of the Crazydomains.com.au website, Dreamscape Networks International, has paid $56,340 in penalties after the ACCC issued it with three infringement notices for allegedly making false or misleading representations about two ‘free’ products automatically added at checkout, and about the benefits of its Domain Privacy product.

records.title: Expecting a delivery during the holiday sales? Stop and think, don’t click the link
records.summary: Australians are being warned to be alert this holiday season to scammers sending text messages and emails impersonating Australia Post and other well-known courier services.

records.title: The ACCC's holiday operating hours
records.summary: The ACCC will have limited operations during the Christmas and New Year period and we wanted to keep you informed of our operations during this time.

records.title: ACCC responds to merger reform proposals
records.summary: Australian consumers and businesses will pay higher prices and have less choice if anti-competitive mergers are able to continue to proceed, the ACCC has warned in a submission to the government’s Competition policy review.

records.title: Airbnb to pay $15m in penalties and up to $15m compensation for misleading consumers
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The Federal Court has ordered Airbnb Ireland UC (Airbnb) to pay $15 million in penalties, and Airbnb will offer up to $15 million in compensation to eligible consumers, after Airbnb admitted it misled consumers about the currency of the prices on its accommodation platform.

records.title: Crusader Caravans pays penalties for allegedly misleading consumers about waterproof testing
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: CCMSM Manufacturing Pty Ltd, trading as Crusader Caravans, has paid $33,000 in penalties after the ACCC issued it with two infringement notices for allegedly making false or misleading representations about waterproofing tests conducted on caravans it manufactured.

records.title: Australia Post to pay about $2.9 million in compensation to businesses for lost or damaged parcels
records.summary: Australia Post and its subsidiary StarTrack have admitted they likely engaged in misleading or deceptive conduct when they failed to accept compensation requests and incorrectly advised some business customers that no compensation was payable to them for lost or damaged articles for four years until October 2022.

records.title: ACCC opposes Australian Clinical Labs’ proposed acquisition of Healius
records.summary: The ACCC has decided to oppose the proposed acquisition of Healius Limited (ASX:HLS) by Australian Clinical Labs Limited (ASX:ACL).

records.title: ACCC proposes to deny authorisation to Bakers Delight to implement price tiers in new point-of-sale system
records.summary: On 14 December 2023, the ACCC released a draft determination proposing to deny authorisation to Bakers Delight Holdings Ltd to implement a price tiering system for a range of promotional products in its new point-of-sale system.

records.title: Bank customers missing out on earning more interest from savings
records.summary: Consumers are missing out on earning higher interest on their savings due to ongoing barriers to searching for, and switching between, retail deposit products, the ACCC’s Retail deposits inquiry final report has found.

records.title: Competition in retail electricity market not delivering for all customers
records.summary: Many Australian households are on a more expensive electricity plan than they need to be, and changes are needed to improve competition in the electricity market and deliver better outcomes for consumers.

records.title: Franchisors warned to remove unfair contract terms or risk legal action
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is warning franchisors to urgently review and amend their standard form franchise agreements or be prepared for potential enforcement action after a review of franchising contracts found wide-ranging concerns.

records.title: Gas supply sufficient for 2024 but smaller surplus forecast
records.summary: The ACCC’s latest gas inquiry report forecasts balanced supply and demand in 2024 if Queensland’s LNG producers export all their currently uncontracted gas, or a 71 petajoule (PJ) surplus if they only export their currently anticipated spot sales.

records.title: Honda to pay $6m in penalties for misleading consumers about dealership closures
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The Federal Court today decided it would impose penalties totalling $6 million on Honda Australia Pty Ltd, accepting Honda engaged in misleading or deceptive conduct, and made false or misleading representations to customers of three former authorised Honda Australia dealerships.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise collective tendering for Southern Sydney Regional Organisation of Councils
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation to Southern Sydney Regional Organisation of Councils and its 12 member councils to jointly tender for the processing of commingled recycling in yellow-lidded kerbside bins and disposal of residuals, and enter into and give effect to contracts that may be executed as an outcome of the procurement conduct.

records.title: Endeavour's proposed acquisition of the Prince Consort Hotel raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has published a Statement of Issues outlining preliminary competition concerns with Endeavour Group Limited’s proposed acquisition of the Prince Consort Hotel in Brisbane’s Fortitude Valley.

records.title: Mattress supplier Emma Sleep in court for alleged misrepresentations about prices
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has instituted proceedings in the Federal Court against Emma Sleep GmbH, and two of its subsidiaries, Bettzeit Southeast Asia Inc and Emma Sleep Pty Ltd (together: Emma Sleep) for alleged false and misleading representations in the advertising of its mattresses, bed frames, pillows and accessories.

records.title: The ACCC has granted authorisation to 6 Victorian councils to jointly procure recycling collection and processing services
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a final determination granting authorisation to the City of Greater Geelong in respect of the following waste services

records.title: Viva Energy's proposed acquisition of OTR Group not opposed, subject to divestiture
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Viva Energy’s (ASX: VEA) proposed acquisition of OTR Group, after accepting a court-enforceable undertaking that commits Viva Energy to divest 25 Coles Express sites in South Australia.

records.title: Woolworths' acquisition of controlling interest in Petstock not opposed, as Petstock gives undertakings relating to past acquisitions
records.summary: The ACCC has accepted a court-enforceable undertaking from Petstock Pty Ltd (Petstock) to divest a package of sites and assets, including 41 retail stores, following the ACCC’s enforcement investigation into past acquisitions by Petstock.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise AMA NSW to collectively bargain with NSW Health and public health organisations
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to revoke authorisation A91383 and grant authorisation AA1000652 in substitution to enable the Australian Medical Association (NSW) Limited (AMA NSW) to continue to collectively bargain on behalf of visiting medical officers engaged in the NSW public hospital system, with NSW Health and public health organisations.

records.title: Delicia Franchising admits breaches of Franchising Code
records.summary: After our investigation, health food and beverage retailer Delicia Franchising has admitted in an undertaking to us that it breached the Franchising Code.

records.title: Stevedoring industry profits remain elevated as competition concerns emerge
records.summary: The operating profit margin of Australia’s container stevedoring industry increased to 24.9 per cent in 2022-23, continuing a trend of rising profit margins since a low of 5.8 per cent in 2018-19, the ACCC’s Container Stevedoring Monitoring Report 2022-23 shows.

records.title: ACCC releases eight principles to guide businesses’ environmental claims
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has today published eight principles to help businesses ensure any environmental marketing and advertising claims they make about their products or services are clear and accurate, and do not mislead consumers.

records.title: Broadband performance in smaller towns nears metro levels
records.summary: Consumers in both urban and regional Australia on NBN fixed-line broadband services have seen an improvement to their download and upload speeds since February 2022, according to the ACCC’s latest Measuring Broadband Australia report.

records.title: Fitbit to pay $11m in penalties for misrepresentations about consumer guarantee rights
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court has ordered US-based Fitbit LLC to pay penalties of $11 million after it admitted making false, misleading or deceptive representations to 58 consumers about their consumer guarantee rights to a refund or a replacement after they claimed their device was faulty.

records.title: Major car parts supplier and retailers pay penalties for alleged breaches of button battery standard
records.summary: Repco, Supercheap Auto and Innovative Mechatronics Group (IMG) (an automotive electrical parts supplier), have each paid penalties after the ACCC issued them with infringement notices for supplying aftermarket car key remotes that allegedly breached warning requirements for products powered by button batteries.

records.title: Consumers and small businesses to benefit from proposed new regulation of digital platforms
records.summary: The ACCC has welcomed the Australian Government’s in-principle agreement to new competition and consumer laws recommended by the ACCC to address harms caused by digital platforms.

records.title: Insurance prices in northern Australia remain high while cyclone reinsurance pool in transition
records.summary: Home, strata and small business insurance premiums in northern Australia remained much more expensive in 2022–23 compared to the rest of Australia, the ACCC’s second annual insurance monitoring report has revealed.

records.title: International factors drove retail fuel prices higher
records.summary: Higher international refined fuel prices driven by OPEC oil production cuts and stronger global oil demand combined with a lower Australian/US dollar exchange rate, contributed to higher petrol prices in the September quarter.

records.title: ACCC grants authorisation for The Hospitals Contribution Fund of Australia's pricing arrangement provisions
records.summary: The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) granted authorisation to health insurer The Hospitals Contribution Fund of Australia Limited (HCF)

records.title: Endeavour's proposed acquisition of Rye Hotel not opposed
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Endeavour Group Limited’s proposed acquisition of the Rye Hotel on the Mornington Peninsula in Victoria.

records.title: Scrutiny of influencers and businesses for misleading advertising and online reviews continues
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The ACCC is providing an update on the next steps in its ongoing work to scrutinise misleading online reviews and influencer endorsements.

records.title: Industry allowed to collaborate to facilitate sustainable access to cash
records.summary: The ACCC has granted interim authorisation to the Australian Banking Association (ABA), its member banks, and other relevant industry participants to discuss and develop arrangements to maintain the physical distribution of cash throughout the Australian economy.

records.title: GetFresh pays penalties for alleged Horticulture Code breaches
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Industry Codes, Agriculture
records.summary: Fresh fruit and vegetable supplier GetFresh Merchants Pty Ltd (GetFresh) has paid penalties totalling $43,150 after the ACCC issued it with three infringement notices for alleged contraventions of the Horticulture Code of Conduct.

records.title: Coles' proposed acquisition of two Saputo milk processing plants not opposed
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Coles’ (ASX: COL) proposed acquisition of two milk processing plants from dairy processor, Saputo.

records.title: Record penalty for resale price maintenance conduct by power tool supplier Techtronic
records.summary: Power tool supplier Techtronic has been ordered to pay penalties totalling $15 million after admitting it had engaged in resale price maintenance conduct in relation to Milwaukee branded products, including power tools, hand tools and accessories.

records.title: ACCC grants authorisation for electricity industry coordination
records.summary: The ACCC has granted authorisation for the Australian Energy Market Operator (AEMO) and electricity industry participants in the National Electricity Market to coordinate the scheduling of repairs, maintenance, renewals upgrades and new connections and associated information sharing.

records.title: Scams Awareness Week 2023 empowers consumers to ask "Who's really there?"
records.summary: Australians are being encouraged to slow down and ask if they really know who they are communicating with, as scammers get better at impersonating businesses and organisations, robbing consumers of $92 million this year.

records.title: Views sought on Airservices Australia's proposed price increase
records.summary: The ACCC is seeking views from the aviation industry and other interested parties on Airservices Australia’s draft proposal to increase the prices it charges airlines for its enroute navigation, terminal navigation, and aviation rescue and fire-fighting (ARFF) services.

records.title: Riff Raff pays penalties after admitting that ads for infant sleep aid toys likely breach consumer law
records.summary: Online national retailer Riff Raff Baby Pty Ltd has paid penalties of $132,000 after the ACCC issued it with eight infringement notices for allegedly making false or misleading statements about its comforter toys being safe for sleep from birth.

records.title: MOO Premium Foods gives undertaking after ACCC investigates 'ocean plastic' claims
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement, Food and Groceries
records.summary: The ACCC has accepted a court-enforceable undertaking from yoghurt manufacturer MOO Premium Foods Pty Ltd (MOO) following an investigation into MOO’s ‘100% ocean plastic’ representations on its yoghurt packaging, website, and social media pages.

records.title: New competition laws needed in response to expansion of digital platforms
records.summary: The continued expansion of Amazon, Apple, Google, Meta and Microsoft into emerging technologies and other markets demonstrates the critical need for regulatory reform, the ACCC has found in its latest report published today.

records.title: Express Online Training in court for allegedly misleading students
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The ACCC has started legal proceedings in the Federal Court against training provider RSA Express (t/a Express Online Training) for allegedly misleading conduct about its online training courses which were offered for students to attain certificates for the responsible service of alcohol (RSA) and construction induction training (White Card).

records.title: Digital platform regulators release working papers on algorithms and AI
records.summary: The Digital Platform Regulators Forum (DP-REG) has published working papers on algorithms and the large language models (LLMs) used in generative artificial intelligence (AI) to mark the launch of its website.

records.title: Consumers urged to immediately check for recalled LG solar batteries after Government safety warning notice
records.summary: Following a recommendation by the ACCC, the Assistant Treasurer, the Hon.

records.title: Shopping for online bargains this Black Friday weekend? Make sure it's the real deal
records.summary: Consumers are being warned to watch out for scam websites impersonating high-profile Australian retailers when shopping online this Black Friday, Cyber Monday sale weekend (24-27 November 2023).

records.title: ACCC consults on Viva Energy's divestiture proposal for OTR acquisition
records.summary: Viva Energy (ASX: VEA) has offered a court-enforceable divestiture proposal to the ACCC in relation to its proposed acquisition of the OTR Group.

records.title: Minimum advertised price proposal for Graco paint sprayers not allowed
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions, Pricing
records.summary: The ACCC has decided to revoke the resale price maintenance notification lodged by Graco Australia Pty Ltd on 21 March 2023.

records.title: The ACCC proposes to authorise joint procurement of recycling collection services for 6 Victorian councils
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC has released a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation to 6 Victorian Councils to engage in collective procurement conduct, and give effect to service contracts that may be executed as an outcome of procurement conduct.

records.title: New international network formed to promote competitive markets in the Pacific
records.summary: The ACCC and its counterpart competition, consumer protection, and economic regulators from across the Pacific will share information, investigative techniques, and authority best practice, in a newly formed initiative to be called the Pacific Island Network of Competition Consumer and Economic Regulators or PINCCER.

records.title: NBN retailers on notice as Australians urged to shop around for deals
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Pricing, Regulated Infrastructure, Telecommunications and Internet
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is urging Australian households to shop around for their NBN broadband plans and check for any offers that represent better value for them, following NBN Co notifying retailers of changes to the wholesale prices that it will charge them from next month.

records.title: Jeep distributor to improve complaints handling for consumer guarantee claims
records.summary: The ACCC has accepted an undertaking by Jeep importer and distributor, Stellantis Australia, to improve how it handles consumer complaints in line with consumer guarantee rights after issues such as multiple repairs or lengthy delays.

records.title: Qantas and China Eastern withdraw application to continue coordination between Australia and China
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: On 24 October 2023, Qantas Airways Limited and China Eastern Airlines Corporation Ltd withdrew their application for revocation of authorisation AA1000526 and substitution for a new authorisation AA1000624 for Qantas and China Eastern to coordinate operations between Australia and mainland China under an Extended Joint Coordination Agreement.

records.title: Mobile tower access may be limiting regional mobile coverage expansion
records.summary: The sale of mobile towers by Australia’s three mobile network operators to large specialist tower companies has changed the structure of the mobile telecommunications industry and made the regulatory regime for tower access no longer fit for purpose, the ACCC’s Regional Mobile Infrastructure Inquiry final report has found.

records.title: Nutrano pays penalties for alleged Horticulture Code breaches
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Industry Codes, Agriculture
records.summary: Fruit grower and trader Seven Fields Operations Pty Ltd, trading as Nutrano, has paid penalties totalling $24,850 for alleged contraventions of the Horticulture Code, after the ACCC issued it with two infringement notices.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise pricing arrangement provisions for The Hospitals Contribution Fund of Australia Limited
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation for 5 years to health insurer The Hospitals Contribution Fund of Australia Limited (HCF).

records.title: Brilliant Lighting admits to engaging in resale price maintenance
records.summary: The ACCC has accepted a court-enforceable undertaking from wholesale lighting and electrical supplier, Brilliant Lighting (Aust) Pty Ltd in which it has admitted that it engaged in resale price maintenance and committed to sending corrective notices to retailers and distributors.

records.title: ACCC consults on Petstock's proposed divestitures to address concerns over completed acquisitions
records.summary: The ACCC is seeking views on a proposed divestiture offered by PETstock Pty Limited in relation to its completed acquisitions of Best Friends Pets, Pet City, Animal Tuckerbox and Pet and Aquarium Warehouse in Eltham, Victoria.

records.title: New NBN regulation will promote competition and long-term interests of Australians
records.summary: The ACCC has accepted NBN Co’s latest proposed variation to its Special Access Undertaking (SAU), which includes measures designed to protect consumers from sharp price rises, reduce barriers to entry for new retailers and create incentives to fix systemic issues that drive poor NBN consumer experience.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise Australian banks to collaborate on development of industry standards to combat scams
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation with conditions until 30 June 2024 for Australian Banking Association Ltd (ABA) and its member banks to participate in discussions to develop an industry standard to prevent, detect, and disrupt scams affecting individual and small business customers.

records.title: ACCC authorises conduct to collaborate on Adblue supply
records.summary: The ACCC has granted authorisation AA1000622, with conditions, to enable AdBlue manufacturers and other industry participants to collaborate, in conjunction with the Australian Government, for specified purposes related to securing sufficient supply of technical grade urea and prioritising access to technical grade urea and diesel exhaust fluid.

records.title: ACCC not satisfied authorisation of coordination between electricity industry participants is necessary
records.summary: The ACCC is proposing in a draft determination to deny authorisation for the Australian Energy Market Operator (AEMO) and electricity industry participants in the National Electricity Market to coordinate the scheduling of repairs, maintenance, renewals upgrades and new connections and associated information sharing.

records.title: Caravan industry warned against misleading price and caravan weight claims
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Pricing, Cars and Vehicles
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is putting the caravan industry on notice that the use of misleading representations in advertising is in breach of the Australian Consumer Law and may result in strong enforcement action being taken against retailers.

records.title: Tesla pays penalties for alleged breach of button battery safety standards
records.summary: Car manufacturer Tesla Motors Australia Pty Ltd has paid penalties of $155,460 after the ACCC issued it with ten infringement notices for allegedly failing to comply with mandatory safety standards for products powered by button batteries, in breach of the Australian Consumer Law.

records.title: ACCC authorises Brookfield and MidOcean’s acquisition of Origin
records.summary: The ACCC has granted authorisation with conditions for the proposed acquisition of Origin Energy (ASX: ORG) by Brookfield and MidOcean.

records.title: Consumers warned to switch off potentially dangerous LG solar batteries immediately
records.summary: All households with a solar storage system need to urgently check if the system has a recalled LG battery installed and should switch off affected batteries immediately, the ACCC has warned.

records.title: Consumers urged to use and store lithium-ion batteries safely to prevent deadly fires
records.summary: The ACCC is warning consumers about rare but serious fire hazards from lithium-ion batteries and is asking consumers to choose, check, use and dispose of the batteries safely, in its latest report published today

records.title: ACCC consults on ACL's proposed divestiture remedy for Healius acquisition
records.summary: The ACCC is seeking views on a proposed court-enforceable undertaking offered by Australian Clinical Labs Limited (ACL) in relation to its proposed acquisition of Healius Limited.

records.title: Review of childcare policy to better meet the needs of families
records.summary: Market forces alone have failed to meet community expectations and government objectives for childcare services and policy, the ACCC’s Childcare inquiry second interim report has found.

records.title: 2023 review of the ACCC's Compliance and Enforcement Policy
records.summary: The ACCC has released a survey to seek feedback from consumers, businesses, and other stakeholders as part of the annual review its Compliance and Enforcement Policy.

records.title: EnergyAustralia in court over alleged misleading electricity price information
records.summary: The ACCC has today instituted proceedings in the Federal Court against EnergyAustralia for allegedly breaching the Electricity Retail Code and the Australian Consumer Law when notifying its customers of impending price changes.

records.title: ACCC opposes Transurban's EastLink acquisition proposal
records.summary: The ACCC has decided to oppose the proposed acquisition of a majority interest in Horizon Roads by Transurban Group (ASX: TCL) after concluding that the transaction would be likely to substantially lessen competition for future toll road concessions in Victoria.

records.title: Endeavour's proposed acquisition of Rye Hotel raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has outlined preliminary competition concerns with Endeavour Group Limited’s proposed acquisition of the Rye Hotel in Rye, Victoria.

records.title: National Anti-Scam Centre warns of spike in scams threatening Chinese students
records.summary: Scammers are increasingly using a range of alarming tactics to threaten, intimidate and steal from Chinese students living in Australia, the National Anti-Scam Centre has warned.

records.title: Gas supply outlook for first quarter 2024 shows likely surplus
records.summary: Australia’s east coast gas market should have a 1.4 petajoule (PJ) surplus in the first quarter of 2024, even if the LNG producers export all their uncontracted gas, the ACCC’s September 2023 interim gas inquiry report shows.

records.title: ACCC proposes to deny authorisation for arrangements to establish the Mortgage Aggregator Assurance Program
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to deny authorisation to the Commonwealth Bank, Westpac, ANZ, NAB and Macquarie Bank to establish a voluntary program for participating mortgage lenders to jointly procure assurance reviews of the compliance systems and standards of participating mortgage aggregators.

records.title: Smaller broadband providers continue to grow their market share
records.summary: Vocus and Aussie Broadband gained almost 62,000 services in the June 2023 quarter, while the total number of residential broadband services on the NBN remained steady, the ACCC’s latest NBN Wholesale Market Indicators Report has found.

records.title: The ACCC proposes to deny coordination between Qantas and China Eastern
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to deny authorisation for Qantas Airways Ltd and China Eastern Airlines Corporation Limited and their related entities (including Jetstar) to continue coordinating operations between Australia and mainland China.

records.title: Diesel prices continued to decrease and were lower than average petrol prices in June 2023
records.summary: Average retail diesel prices in Australia’s five largest cities (Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Adelaide, Perth) continued to decrease during the June quarter 2023 to similar levels as average retail petrol prices, the ACCC’s latest petrol monitoring report shows.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise conduct to collaborate on Adblue supply
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation with conditions for 4 years to AdBlue manufacturers and other industry participants to collaborate, in conjunction with the Australian Government, for specified purposes related to securing sufficient supply of technical grade urea and prioritising access to technical grade urea and diesel exhaust fluid.

records.title: Businesses urged to remove unfair contract terms ahead of law changes
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is encouraging businesses to review their standard form contracts and remove or amend any unfair contract terms before new penalties take effect.

records.title: Digital platform regulators make joint submission on AI
records.summary: In a joint submission to the Department of Industry, Science and Resources (DISR) consultation on the ‘Safe and responsible AI in Australia’ Discussion Paper, members of the Digital Platform Regulators forum (DP-REG) have outlined the opportunities and challenges presented by rapid advances in artificial intelligence (AI).

records.title: Broadband services provide good connectivity to popular business applications
records.summary: Key NBN broadband services used by small and medium sized Australian businesses typically provide good support to popular business applications, particularly those hosted within Australia, the ACCC’s latest Measuring Broadband Australia report has found.

records.title: ACCC court action against eHarmony for alleged misleading online dating membership statements
records.summary: The ACCC has today commenced proceedings in the Federal Court against dating site eHarmony Inc, alleging it breached the Australian Consumer Law by making misleading statements about the pricing, renewal and duration of its memberships.

records.title: Swift Networks to pay $1.2 m penalty for rigging bids for WA mining camps tenders
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions
records.summary: The Federal Court has today ordered Swift Networks Pty Ltd (Swift) to pay a penalty of $1.2 million for engaging in cartel conduct by rigging bids when tendering to supply technology infrastructure and services to three Pilbara mining village projects.

records.title: ACCC calls for views on Australia Post's proposed price increase
records.summary: The ACCC is seeking views on Australia Post’s draft proposal to increase its basic postage rate.

records.title: Parents and guardians survey now available in Chinese, Arabic, Korean and Vietnamese
records.summary: The ACCC is asking parents and guardians from culturally and linguistically diverse communities to share their views on childcare services in Australia, as part of its year-long inquiry into the sector.

records.title: ACCC welcomes consultation on possible unfair trading practices regulatory reforms
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The ACCC welcomes the Australian Government launching consultation on possible reforms to the Australian Consumer Law to address currently unregulated unfair trading practices.

records.title: ACCC takes court action alleging Qantas advertised flights it had already cancelled
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement, Travel and Airports
