records.summary: The ACCC today launched action in the Federal Court of Australia alleging Qantas Airways (QAN) engaged in false, misleading or deceptive conduct, by advertising tickets for more than 8,000 flights that it had already cancelled but not removed from sale.

records.title: Proposal to set minimum advertised prices on paint spraying equipment raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to revoke a resale price maintenance notification lodged by paint spraying equipment distributor, Graco Australia, which wants to set a minimum advertised price for its range of airless and air assisted paint sprayers.

records.title: Pet Circle pays penalties for allegedly misleading customers about cost of online goods
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: Millell Pty Ltd, trading as Pet Circle, has paid penalties of $26,640 after the ACCC issued it with two infringement notices for allegedly making false or misleading representations on its website to two customers about the price of goods at ‘checkout’ when completing a purchase of pet supplies using the Pet Circle website.

records.title: Record $57.5 million penalty for BlueScope’s attempted price fixing
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions
records.summary: Steel manufacturer BlueScope Steel has been ordered to pay a $57.5 million penalty for attempting to fix prices for flat steel products supplied in Australia.

records.title: Consultation commences on NBN Co's revised Special Access Undertaking variation
records.summary: The ACCC is inviting submissions from the telecommunications industry, consumer groups and other interested parties on NBN Co’s revised Special Access Undertaking (SAU) variation proposal, in a consultation paper published today.

records.title: Fertiliser suppliers amend unfair contract terms after ACCC investigation
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Agriculture
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Suppliers in the fertiliser industry have agreed to amend their contracts following an ACCC investigation into unfair contract terms.

records.title: Qantas and Emirates to continue coordination on flights
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: The ACCC has granted authorisation for Qantas Airways Ltd, Emirates and their related entities (including Jetstar) to continue coordinating their passenger and cargo transport operations across their networks until 2028. These networks cover routes between Australia and the UK/Europe, New Zealand, Asia, the Middle East and North Africa.

records.title: NBN Co submits revised variation to its Special Access Undertaking
records.summary: NBN Co has withdrawn its proposed November 2022 variation to its Special Access Undertaking (SAU) and simultaneously submitted a revised variation proposal to the ACCC.

records.title: Compliance guidelines for gas industry as new Gas Market Code takes effect
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Industry Codes, Energy
records.summary: The ACCC has today published compliance and enforcement guidelines on the new Gas Market Code.

records.title: Scam alert - National Anti-Scam Centre warns of scams targeting Matildas fans
records.summary: Matildas fans are being urged to be wary of scammers ahead of Wednesday night’s FIFA Women’s World Cup semi-final.

records.title: $10m penalty for Dell Australia for misleading representations about discount prices of computer monitors
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Pricing, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The Federal Court today ordered that Dell Australia Pty Ltd pay $10 million for making false and misleading representations on its website about discount prices for add-on computer monitors.

records.title: Airports' financial results in 2021-22 impacted by COVID-19 but passengers returned
records.summary: The financial performance of Australia’s four largest airports (Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane and Perth) in 2021-22 remained well below the results achieved in the last full non-pandemic financial year of 2018-19, the ACCC’s latest Airport Monitoring Report shows.

records.title: Regulated water charges fell in real terms across most Basin states in 2021-22
records.summary: Regulated water charges varied substantially between the different states in the Murray-Darling Basin in 2021-22 as wet conditions across the Basin kept water availability high, the ACCC’s annual Water Monitoring Report 2021-22 shows.

records.title: ACCC grants authorisation for Health Partners' pricing arrangement provisions
records.summary: On 10 August 2023, the ACCC granted authorisation to health insurer Health Partners to enter into and give effect to certain pricing arrangement provisions in agreements with dentists and dental practices which will form part of its partner network of dental practices in regional South Australia.

records.title: Australians urged to compare electricity plans as market offers exceed safety net
records.summary: The ACCC is urging households and small businesses to contact their energy company and ask if a cheaper electricity plan is available, in light of some recent price increases in the range of 10 to 20 per cent above the regulated safety net.

records.title: ACCC denies authorisation for ANZ to acquire Suncorp Bank
records.summary: The ACCC has decided not to grant merger authorisation for ANZ Banking Group (ASX:ANZ) to acquire Suncorp Group’s (ASX:SUN) banking arm.

records.title: Australian banks allowed to collaborate on development of industry standards to combat scams
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Banking and Finance, Stay Protected, Scams
records.summary: The Australian Banking Association Ltd (ABA) and its member banks will be able to participate in discussions to develop an industry standard to prevent, detect, and disrupt scams affecting individual and small business customers, following a conditional ACCC interim authorisation.

records.title: Hornet Industries admits to likely resale price maintenance
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: Recreational bike importer and distributor, Hornet Industries Pty Ltd, has admitted that it likely engaged in resale price maintenance.

records.title: Court finds Delta and its director attempted to rig National Gallery tender
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions
records.summary: The Federal Court has found that Delta Building Automation Ltd (Delta) and its sole director, Timothy Davis attempted to rig a bid in connection with a tender conducted by the National Gallery of Australia for the replacement and ongoing maintenance of its building management system.

records.title: Cotton Seed Distributors removes potentially anti-competitive restraints in agency agreements
records.category: Agriculture, Competition and Exemptions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The sole supplier of cotton planting seed in Australia, Cotton Seed Distributors Ltd (CSD), has provided a court-enforceable undertaking to address ACCC concerns about potentially anti-competitive terms in its agreements with agents.

records.title: Record penalties of $438m ordered against Phoenix Institute and CTI for acting unconscionably and misleading students
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court has today imposed record penalties of $438m against former vocational college Phoenix Institute of Australia Pty Ltd (Phoenix) and its marketing arm Community Training Initiatives Pty Ltd (CTI) in proceedings brought by the ACCC and the Commonwealth.

records.title: $20m penalty for Meta companies for conduct liable to mislead consumers about use of their data
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement, Digital Platforms and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court has ordered two subsidiaries of social media giant Meta, Facebook Israel and Onavo Inc, to each pay $10 million for engaging in conduct liable to mislead in breach of the Australian Consumer Law, in an action brought by the ACCC.

records.title: Lactalis penalised for Dairy Code breaches
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Industry Codes, Agriculture
records.summary: The Federal Court today ordered Lactalis Australia Pty Ltd (Lactalis) to pay $950,000 in penalties for contravening the Dairy Code of Conduct by failing to meet some of its obligations in relation to the 2020-21 milk season.

records.title: Scam alert - new scam targets customers of loyalty points programs
records.summary: A new scam targeting customers of loyalty points programs with large, well-known Australian companies has been detected by the National Anti-Scam Centre.

records.title: Costco pays penalties for alleged misleading lobster country of origin claims
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Agriculture, Food and Groceries, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: Costco Wholesale Australia Pty Ltd has paid penalties totalling $33,000 after the ACCC issued it with two infringement notices for alleged false or misleading labelling of the country and place of origin on lobster products and failing to comply with the Country of Origin Food Labelling Information Standard.

records.title: National Anti-Scam Centre brings together cross-sector leaders to stop scammers
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre held its inaugural Advisory Board meeting this week marking the commencement of greater cross-sector collaboration in the fight against scammers.

records.title: ACL's proposed acquisition of Healius raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has outlined significant preliminary competition concerns with Australian Clinical Labs Limited’s (ASX:ACL) proposed acquisition of Healius Limited (ASX:HLS) in a Statement of Issues published today.

records.title: Coles' proposed acquisition of two Saputo milk plants raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has identified preliminary competition concerns about Coles’ (ASX:COL) proposed acquisition of two Saputo milk processing plants located in NSW and Victoria, in a Statement of Issues published today.

records.title: Bache Bros pays penalty for alleged Horticulture Code breach
records.category: Agriculture, Industry Codes, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: Bache Bros Pty Ltd has paid a penalty of $13,750 after the ACCC issued it with an Infringement Notice for an alleged contravention of the Horticulture Code of Conduct.

records.title: ACCC publishes draft guidance to improve businesses' environmental claims
records.summary: The ACCC today published draft guidance to improve the integrity of environmental and sustainability claims made by businesses and protect consumers from ‘greenwashing’.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise pricing arrangement provisions for Health Partners
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation for 5 years to health insurer Health Partners to enter into and give effect to certain pricing arrangement provisions in agreements with dentists and partner dental practices which form part of its partner network of dental practices (Partner Practices) in regional South Australia.

records.title: Interim authorisation for electricity industry to coordinate maintenance
records.summary: The ACCC has granted interim authorisation for the Australian Energy Market Operator (AEMO) and electricity industry participants in the National Electricity Market to collaborate to ensure reliability of electricity supply during Australia’s transition towards renewable electricity.

records.title: ACCC invites views on data broker industry
records.category: Regulated Infrastructure, Digital Platforms and Services
records.summary: The ACCC is calling on consumers, businesses and interested stakeholders to provide submissions about data broker services in Australia, as part of its five-year digital platform services inquiry.

records.title: Pricing practices and operating costs of childcare services to be examined, as latest ACCC report confirms fees outpaced inflation
records.summary: The ACCC has examined the price and availability of childcare services, how households select childcare services and the impact of Government contributions in its interim report, published today.

records.title: Secure Parking in court for alleged misleading claims about 'Secure-a-Spot' service
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Cars and Vehicles, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The ACCC has instituted proceedings in the Federal Court against Secure Parking Pty Ltd for allegedly breaching the Australian Consumer Law by making false or misleading claims about its ‘Secure-a-Spot’ service.

records.title: Communique | Digital Platform Regulators Forum
records.summary: The heads of the four members of the Digital Platform Regulators Forum (DP-REG) met on 20 June 2023 to review the forum’s progress over 2022-23 and to discuss strategic priorities for the year ahead.

records.title: National Anti-Scam Centre's first fusion cell to disrupt investment scams
records.summary: The National Anti-Scam Centre will coordinate an investment scam fusion cell to combat the growing problem of investment scams, which are costing Australians more than $1 billion a year.

records.title: ACCC intervenes to ensure 543 businesses avoid higher electricity bills
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Energy, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Energy retailer Blue NRG has agreed to compensate several small business clients after it admitted it made false or misleading representations when telling more than 500 customers on fixed-rate contracts that it had a legal right to raise electricity prices, when it did not.

records.title: Cooperation to continue on soft plastics recycling after REDcycle collapse
records.summary: The ACCC has granted conditional authorisation to allow the major supermarkets to continue their collaboration to manage the soft plastics stockpile and resume in-store collections after REDcycle suspended its recycling operations.

records.title: Gas supply outlook for 2024 improves but risk of winter shortfalls remains
records.summary: Australia’s east coast gas market is expected to have enough supply to meet overall demand in 2024, the ACCC’s June 2023 interim gas inquiry report reveals.

records.title: Households face higher electricity bills as wholesale price spikes flow through
records.summary: Residential electricity bills were higher across most National Electricity Market states in the September quarter last year compared to the equivalent quarter in 2021, the ACCC’s latest Electricity Market Inquiry Report reveals.

records.title: Australians receive consistent download speeds as fixed wireless connections improve
records.summary: Broadband retailers are continuing to deliver download speeds to consumers that are close to their maximum plan speeds, the ACCC’s latest Measuring Broadband Australia report finds.

records.title: Transurban's proposed majority ownership of Horizon Roads raises concerns
records.summary: The ACCC has raised preliminary competition concerns about Transurban’s (ASX: TCL) proposed acquisition of a majority interest in Horizon Roads Pty Ltd.

records.title: Green Endeavour pays penalty for alleged breach of Horticulture Code
records.category: Agriculture, Industry Codes, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: Fruit and vegetable wholesaler Green Endeavour Pty Ltd has paid a penalty of $13,750 after the ACCC issued it with an infringement notice for an alleged contravention of the Horticulture Code of Conduct.

records.title: ACCC investigates as button battery products fail to comply with information rules to keep children safe
records.summary: More than a third of products containing potentially lethal button batteries failed to include mandatory warnings, a national surveillance program coordinated by the ACCC has revealed.

records.title: CovaU and ReAmped pay penalties for alleged breaches of electricity code
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Industry Codes, Energy
records.summary: Electricity retailers CovaU Pty Ltd and ReAmped Energy Pty Ltd have each paid $33,300 in penalties after the ACCC issued each retailer with three infringement notices for alleged contraventions of the Electricity Retail Code.

records.title: ACCC authorises Live Performance Australia to collectively negotiate for copyright licences on behalf of members
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Telecommunications and Internet
records.summary: The ACCC has granted authorisation AA1000637 to enable Australian Entertainment Industry Association (trading as Live Performance Australia) to negotiate with the Australasian Performing Right Association, the Phonographic Performance Company of Australia and OneMusic Australia, on behalf of members with respect to the acquisition of copyright licences.

records.title: The ACCC proposes to allow Qantas and Emirates coordination to continue
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to authorise Qantas Airways Ltd, Emirates and their related entities (including Jetstar) to continue coordinating operations for five years.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation to Fred IT Group Pty Ltd
records.summary: The ACCC has granted interim authorisation to Fred IT Group to make, but not give effect to, one or more contracts, arrangements or understandings involving MediSecure and its related bodies corporate to facilitate a smooth transition to a sole-provider model for prescription delivery systems for publicly-funded electronic scripts.

records.title: ACCC issues determination on NBN Co's revenue constraints under its current Special Access Undertaking
records.summary: The ACCC has published its determination on NBN Co’s 2021-22 Long Term Revenue Constraint Methodology (LTRCM) under its current Special Access Undertaking (SAU).

records.title: Australian Competition Tribunal denies authorisation for Telstra and TPG regional network deal
records.summary: The Australian Competition Tribunal has affirmed the ACCC’s decision not to grant authorisation for the proposed regional spectrum authorisation arrangements between Telstra Corporation Limited (ASX:TLS and ASX:TL1) and TPG Telecom Limited (ASX:TPG).

records.title: Diesel prices down by 10 per cent in the March quarter as petrol prices remained steady
records.summary: Retail diesel prices dropped significantly in the March quarter 2023 while petrol prices remained broadly stable, which resulted in the gap between diesel and petrol prices narrowing, the ACCC’s latest petrol monitoring report has found.

records.title: ACCC concerns about Mitsubishi discount offer resolved following consumer refunds
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement, Cars and Vehicles
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: Following ACCC concerns that Mitsubishi Motors Australia Limited (Mitsubishi) may have made false or misleading claims to members of the Queensland Farmers’ Federation (QFF) about discounts available to them off the price of vehicles, Mitsubishi has provided partial refunds to affected consumers.

records.title: Young children a top focus for new ACCC product safety priorities
records.summary: Consumer products affecting young children’s safety and infant sleep products are among the ACCC’s product safety priorities during 2023-24, ACCC Deputy Chair Catriona Lowe announced today.

records.title: ACCC authorises Armaguard and Prosegur's merger, subject to undertaking
records.summary: The ACCC has granted authorisation to the proposed merger of Linfox Armaguard Pty Ltd (Armaguard) and Prosegur Australia Holdings Limited and has accepted a court-enforceable undertaking, which is a condition of the authorisation.

records.title: Lower speed internet plans gain popularity while smaller telcos continue to grow market share
records.summary: The number of residential broadband services on the 25 Mbps speed tier grew by 125,000 services, to approximately 1.4 million, or 16 per cent of the NBN wholesale market, in the March 2023 quarter, the ACCC’s latest NBN Wholesale Market Indicators Report reveals.

records.title: Dell Australia declared to have misled consumers about the prices of computer monitors
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Pricing, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The Federal Court has declared by consent that Dell Australia Pty Ltd made false and misleading representations on its website about the prices of add-on monitors and in ‘strikethrough’ prices shown on the website.

records.title: Domestic airline competition at a critical juncture
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: A lack of effective competition in Australia’s domestic airline industry, which has characterised the industry for decades, has resulted in higher airfares and poorer service for consumers, the ACCC’s final Airline Competition in Australia report concludes.

records.title: ACCC begins inquiry into regulation of telecommunications services
records.summary: The ACCC has today commenced a combined public inquiry into whether nine wholesale telecommunications services that support the provision of broadband, voice and data transmission services should continue to be regulated.

records.title: ACCC grants Bulk Wheat Port Code exemption to Commodity Ag at Albany
records.category: Regulated Infrastructure, Rail, Shipping and Ports
records.summary: The ACCC has determined that Commodity Ag is an ‘exempt service provider’ of port terminal services provided via its port terminal facility at Albany, Western Australia.

records.title: Consultation opens on Endeavour's proposed acquisition of Rye Hotel on the Mornington Peninsula
records.summary: The ACCC is seeking views from consumers and businesses on the Mornington Peninsula, about Endeavour Group Limited’s proposed acquisition of the Rye Hotel in Rye, Victoria.

records.title: Tradie platform hipages rectifies subscription trap issues
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: Online home improvement platform hipages Group Pty Ltd (hipages) has admitted it likely engaged in misleading or deceptive conduct in breach of the Australian Consumer Law from October 2018 to January 2022 by failing to adequately disclose contract terms that allowed it to automatically renew subscriptions and charge an early termination fee.

records.title: Families report lengthy waitlists and increasing fees in preliminary survey results
records.summary: Parents and guardians have reported having their return to work delayed due to a lack of childcare while some are struggling with ‘extremely expensive’ fees, in initial responses to an ACCC Parents and guardians survey.

records.title: National online florist Meg's Flowers in court over alleged misleading local florist claims
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The ACCC has instituted proceedings in the Federal Court against Meg’s Flowers Pty Ltd for allegedly breaching the Australian Consumer Law by making false or misleading representations that it was a local florist, when this was not the case.

records.title: ACCC grants authorisation to Odin joint venture for marketing gas
records.summary: The ACCC has granted authorisation for Vintage Energy Ltd, Metgasco Ltd and Bridgeport (Cooper Basin) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of New Hope Corporation Limited, to enter into joint gas marketing arrangements.

records.title: ACCC grants interim authorisation to The Hospitals Contribution Fund of Australia Limited
records.summary: The ACCC has granted interim authorisation to The Hospitals Contribution Fund of Australia Limited (HCF) and current and future dentists who are members of HCF’s More For Teeth program

records.title: ACCC opposes Woolworths' acquisition of SUPA IGA Karabar
records.summary: The ACCC opposes Woolworths' proposed acquisition of the SUPA IGA in Karabar, NSW and its co-located Liquor Boss store, after concluding the transaction would be likely to substantially lessen competition in the supply of groceries in the local area.

records.title: ACCC welcomes reappointment of Deputy Chair Mick Keogh
records.summary: ACCC Chair Gina Cass Gottlieb has warmly welcomed today’s announcement by Treasurer Jim Chalmers that he will reappoint ACCC Deputy Chair Mick Keogh for a further five years, starting from 30 May 2023.

records.title: ACCC grants re-authorisation for intra-practice price setting arrangements for shared dental practices
records.summary: On 25 May 2023, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) granted re-authorisation to the Australian Dental Association Inc (the ADA).

records.title: The ACCC reauthorises coordination of maintenance between LNG producers in WA and NT
records.summary: The ACCC has granted re-authorisation with a condition to Chevron Australia Pty Ltd, INPEX Operations Australia Pty Ltd, Shell Australia Pty Ltd and Woodside Energy Ltd regarding the sequencing and timing of scheduled maintenance at their 6 LNG facilities in Western Australia and Northern Territory.

records.title: ACCC welcomes funding to establish National Anti-Scam Centre
records.summary: The ACCC has welcomed the Australian Government’s commitment to establish a new National Anti-Scam Centre (NASC).

records.title: The ACCC authorises coordination between British Airways, Qatar Airways and Iberia
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: The ACCC has granted authorisation AA1000630 to enable British Airways plc, Qatar Airways Group Q.C.S.C.

records.title: ACCC proposes to allow Live Performance Australia to collectively negotiate for copyright licences on behalf of members
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Telecommunications and Internet
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation AA1000637 to enable Australian Entertainment Industry Association (trading as Live Performance Australia) to negotiate with the Australasian Performing Right Association, the Phonographic Performance Company Australia and OneMusic Australia.

records.title: ACCC denies re-authorisation of airline charter alliance
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: The ACCC has denied Virgin Australia Airlines and Alliance Aviation Services’ (ASX:AQZ) application for re-authorisation of their agreement to coordinate and jointly tender for and supply services to corporate customers, mainly for fly-in fly-out employees.

records.title: ACCC grants Bulk Wheat Port Code exemption to T-Ports at Wallaroo
records.category: Rail, Shipping and Ports, Regulated Infrastructure
records.summary: The ACCC has determined that T-Ports Pty Ltd is an exempt service provider of port terminal services provided via its port terminal facility at Wallaroo, South Australia.

records.title: New Chief Economist Dr Lilla Csorgo starts at ACCC
records.summary: ACCC Chair Gina Cass-Gottlieb has welcomed the appointment of Dr Lilla Csorgo as its new Chief Economist.

records.title: Vitamin and supplement supplier removes misleading statements from Wealthy Health website
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The ACCC has accepted a court-enforceable undertaking from Universal Pharmaceuticals Pty Ltd, admitting that representations on its Wealthy Health website that a product contained squalene from sharks in Australian waters were likely misleading, in breach of the Australian Consumer Law.

records.title: ACCC rejects NBN Co Special Access Undertaking variation
records.summary: After a thorough assessment of NBN Co’s proposed variation to its Special Access Undertaking (SAU), and informed by constructive and extensive engagement with NBN Co, Retail Service Providers, consumer representatives and other stakeholders, the ACCC has today released a draft decision to reject the proposed variation.

records.title: Retailers pay penalties for supplying Halloween products allegedly in breach of button battery standards
records.summary: The Reject Shop and Dusk have paid a total of nearly $240,000 after the ACCC issued them with infringement notices.

records.title: ACCC report on social media reinforces the need for more protections for consumers and small business
records.category: Digital Platforms and Services, Stay Protected, Scams
records.summary: The ACCC continues to support reforms to protect consumers and businesses following a new report into social media services.

records.title: ACCC updates Dairy Code guidance for industry
records.summary: Dairy processors have been provided with additional information on their legal obligations under the Dairy Code of Conduct in advance of the 1 June deadline for the release of new milk supply agreements for the 2023-24 dairy season.

records.title: ACCC seeks views on competition and consumer issues affecting Australian savers
records.category: Banking and Finance, Competition and Exemptions
records.summary: The ACCC is calling on banks, bank customers and other interested stakeholders to share their views on competition and consumer issues affecting retail deposit products.

records.title: ACCC opposes Qantas' acquisition of Alliance
records.summary: The ACCC will oppose Qantas Airways’ (ASX: QAN) proposed acquisition of Alliance Aviation Services Ltd (ASX: AQZ).

records.title: Sika's proposed acquisition of MBCC Group not opposed, subject to global divestiture
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Sika’s proposed acquisition of MBCC Group, after accepting a court-enforceable undertaking that commits Sika to divest MBCC Group’s entire business in Australia and New Zealand, including its subsidiary, Bluey Technologies.

records.title: ACCC re-authorises coordination between Jetstar's Asian brands
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: The ACCC has decided to grant re-authorisation for five years until 11 May 2028 to Qantas Airways Ltd and Jetstar Airways Pty Ltd for the continued coordination of two Jetstar Asian-based joint ventures and, in certain circumstances, between Jetstar Japan and Japan Airlines.

records.title: Parents and guardians asked to share views on childcare
records.summary: The ACCC is asking parents and guardians for their views on childcare services in Australia, as part of its year-long inquiry into the sector.

records.title: ACCC calls for united front as scammers steal over $3bn from Australians
records.summary: The latest Targeting Scams report has revealed Australians lost a record $3.1 billion to scams in 2022, as government, law enforcement and the private sector look to improve collaborative efforts to support the community in the fight against scams.

records.title: ARM Architecture and its former MD to pay penalties for attempted rigging of university tender
records.category: Compliance and Enforcement, Competition and Exemptions
records.summary: The Federal Court today found architecture firm Ashton Raggatt McDougall Pty Ltd (ARM Architecture) and its former managing director, Anthony (Tony) John Allen had attempted to rig bids for a tender relating to a building project at Darwin’s Charles Darwin University. It ordered ARM Architecture and Mr Allen to pay penalties of $900,000 and $75,000

records.title: ACCC grants re-authorisation for back-to-back milk purchasing policies and milk pricing arrangements
records.summary: On 12 April 2023, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) granted re-authorisation to the Dairy Farmers Milk Co-operative Ltd (the Co-operative) to give effect to ‘back- to-back arrangements’ with regard to milk purchasing policies and pricing arrangements.

records.title: ACCC proposes to re-authorise intrapractice price setting arrangements for shared dental practices
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant re-authorisation to the Australian Dental Association Inc (ADA).

records.title: Reform of merger laws critical for Australia's economic transition
records.summary: Australia’s merger laws are no longer fit for purpose and tilted too much towards allowing potentially anti-competitive mergers to proceed, ACCC Chair Gina Cass-Gottlieb said today. In a speech to the National Press Club, Ms Cass-Gottlieb called for reforms to Australia’s merger laws to protect competition in Australia’s economy.

records.title: Captain Cook College appeal against systemic unconscionable conduct finding dismissed
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Full Federal Court has dismissed an appeal by Captain Cook College against a decision in July 2021 that it had engaged in a system of unconscionable conduct to secure additional government funding for online diploma courses under the former VET FEE-HELP loan program.

records.title: Most gamers enjoy fast connections as online gaming experience measured for first time
records.summary: Consumers typically have fast connections to online gaming servers over home broadband connections, with NSW and ACT gamers enjoying the best experience, according to the ACCC’s latest Measuring Broadband Australia report which for the first time measured Australians’ online experience for gaming services.

records.title: ACCC proposes to grant a Bulk Wheat Port Code exemption to Commodity Ag at Albany
records.category: Regulated Infrastructure, Industry Codes, Rail, Shipping and Ports
records.summary: The ACCC has released a draft determination proposing to grant Commodity Ag Pty Ltd exempt service provider status under the Port Terminal Access (Bulk Wheat) Code of Conduct in relation to its port terminal facility at Albany.

records.title: ACCC publishes new product safety recall guidelines
records.summary: The ACCC has published updated guidelines to assist suppliers when they need to recall a product for safety reasons.

records.title: ACCC seeks further views on ANZ's proposed acquisition of Suncorp Bank
records.summary: The ACCC has released a statement of preliminary views on ANZ Banking Group’s (ASX:ANZ) proposed acquisition of Suncorp Group’s (ASX:SUN) banking arm.

records.title: Gas shortfall in 2023 less likely as gas supply outlook improves
records.summary: The ACCC’s March 2023 interim gas inquiry report has found there should be sufficient gas supply in the east coast gas market against forecast demand if LNG producers contract another 3 petajoules (PJ) of gas in 2023.

records.title: Bank impersonation scams robbing Australians of their life savings
records.category: Stay Protected, Scams, Banking and Finance
records.summary: Consumers are being warned to be wary of phone calls and texts that appear to be from their bank, following alarming reports of Australians losing their life savings to a highly sophisticated impersonation scam.

records.title: Cooperation proposed to continue on soft plastics recycling after REDcycle liquidation
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant authorisation with conditions for 12 months to allow the major supermarkets to continue collaborating on a short-term solution to manage the soft plastics stockpile and to facilitate the resumption of in-store collections for recycling.

records.title: Interim authorisation granted to Qantas and China Eastern
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: Qantas Airways, China Eastern and their related entities can continue coordinating their passenger and cargo transport operations following an interim authorisation from the ACCC.

records.title: ACCC grants authorisation for joint tender of renewable electricity services by group of organisations in Victoria
records.summary: On 29 March 2023, the ACCC granted authorisation to 1Circle Pty Ltd and members of the Business Renewables Buying Group to enable them to form a joint renewable energy buying group.

records.title: The ACCC has granted authorisation to Bayside Council and Georges River Council
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a final determination granting authorisation to Bayside Council and Georges River Council to jointly tender and enter into contracts for waste and recycling services, and enter into separate contracts for these services.

records.title: The ACCC proposes to authorise coordination between British Airways, Qatar Airways and Iberia
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: The ACCC has issued a draft determination proposing to grant authorisation AA1000630 to enable British Airways plc, Qatar Airways Group Q.C.S.C.

records.title: International factors kept diesel prices significantly higher than petrol prices
records.summary: Petrol prices increased slightly and diesel prices remained significantly higher than petrol prices in the December quarter 2022, according to the ACCC’s latest quarterly petrol monitoring report.

records.title: Court dismisses appeals against Mazda judgment
records.category: Buying and Selling Products and Services, Cars and Vehicles, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Full Federal Court today dismissed an ACCC appeal against a Federal Court judgment that Mazda did not engage in unconscionable conduct in its dealings with nine consumers.

records.title: Interim authorisation granted to Qantas and Emirates
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: The ACCC has granted interim authorisation to enable Qantas Airways Ltd, Emirates and their related entities to continue coordinating their passenger and cargo transport operations while the ACCC assesses their substantive application for authorisation.

records.title: ACCC proposes to re-authorise back-to-back milk purchasing policies and milk pricing arrangements
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to grant re-authorisation to Dairy Farmers Milk Co-operative (the Co-operative) to give effect to back-to-back arrangements between the Co-operative, its members and Bega.

records.title: ACCC proposes to re-authorise coordination of maintenance between LNG producers in WA and NT
records.summary: The ACCC is proposing to grant re-authorisation with a condition to Chevron Australia Pty Ltd, INPEX Operations Australia Pty Ltd, Shell Australia Pty Ltd and Woodside Energy Ltd regarding the sequencing and timing of scheduled maintenance at their 6 LNG facilities in Western Australia and Northern Territory for another 5 years.

records.title: Booktopia to pay $6m for misleading statements about consumer guarantee rights
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Buying and Selling Products and Services, Compliance and Enforcement
categories: Buying and Selling Products and Services
records.summary: The Federal Court has today ordered Booktopia Pty Ltd (Booktopia) pay $6 million in penalties for making false or misleading representations on its website, and in dealings with consumers, about consumer guarantee rights.

records.title: Domestic airfares fall but prices remain above pre-pandemic levels
records.summary: Domestic airfares have declined from historic highs at the end of 2022; however, they remain above 2019 prices, the ACCC’s latest Airline Competition in Australia report reveals.

records.title: Expanding digital platform ecosystems to be examined by ACCC
records.summary: The ACCC will examine the expanding ecosystems of digital platform service providers in Australia as part of its’ five-year Digital Platform Services Inquiry.

records.title: Competition and consumer issues in essential services, sustainability among 2023-24 compliance and enforcement priorities
records.summary: Consumer and competition issues in essential services, environmental claims and sustainability, financial services and other critical areas will be among the ACCC’s compliance and enforcement priorities during 2023-24, ACCC Chair Gina Cass-Gottlieb announced today.

records.title: Interim authorisation granted to Jetstar's Asian brands
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Travel and Airports
records.summary: The ACCC has granted interim authorisation to Qantas Airways Ltd (ASX:QAN) and Jetstar Airways Pty Ltd for the continued coordination of two Jetstar Asian-based joint ventures and, in certain circumstances between Jetstar Japan and Japan Airlines.

records.title: Residential broadband market flattens as smaller telcos gain wholesale market share
records.summary: The number of residential NBN services declined for the first time in the December 2022 quarter, by 0.1 per cent - or almost 9,000 - to approximately 8.73 million, the ACCC’s latest NBN Wholesale Market Indicators Report reveals.

records.title: ACCC 'greenwashing' internet sweep unearths widespread concerning claims
records.summary: The ACCC will be investigating a number of businesses for potential ‘greenwashing’, following an internet sweep which found more than half of the businesses reviewed made concerning claims about their environmental or sustainability practices.

records.title: ACCC proposes to authorise joint tender of renewable electricity services by group of organisations in Victoria
records.summary: The ACCC proposes to authorise 1Circle Pty Ltd and members of the Business Renewables Buying Group (the Group) to form a joint renewable energy buying group in which the Group pools their electricity demand and conducts a joint procurement and negotiation process for the supply of electricity from renewable energy sources.

records.title: Aussie Skips and CEO plead guilty to alleged waste services price fixing cartel
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: Sydney waste companies, Aussie Skips Recycling and Aussie Skips Bin Services (together Aussie Skips), along with its chief executive Emmanuel Roussakis have today each pleaded guilty in the Federal Court to a criminal cartel offence following charges laid by the Commonwealth Director of Public Prosecutions (CDPP) last December.

records.title: ACCC publishes submissions on proposed variation to future NBN regulation
records.summary: The ACCC has today published the submissions received about NBN Co’s proposed variation to its Special Access Undertaking.

records.title: Product Safety Pledge removes thousands of dangerous items from online marketplaces
records.summary: More than 15,000 potentially unsafe products were delisted by online marketplaces signed up to the ACCC’s Australian Product Safety Pledge last financial year, with 98 per cent of regulator-initiated take down requests actioned within 2 days.

records.title: Endeavour's proposed acquisition of Beachfront Hotel in Darwin not opposed
records.summary: The ACCC will not oppose Endeavour Group Limited’s proposed acquisition of the Beachfront Hotel in Rapid Creek, just north of Darwin.

records.title: Full Federal Court dismisses ACCC appeal in NSW Ports case
records.category: Regulated Infrastructure, Rail, Shipping and Ports, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The Full Federal Court has dismissed an appeal by the ACCC in its proceedings against NSW Ports.

records.title: ACCC proposes to grant a Bulk Wheat Port Code exemption to T-Ports at Wallaroo
records.category: Regulated Infrastructure, Industry Codes, Rail, Shipping and Ports
records.summary: The ACCC has released a draft determination proposing to grant T-Ports Pty Ltd exempt service provider status under the Port Terminal Access (Bulk Wheat) Code of Conduct in relation to its port terminal facility at Wallaroo.

records.title: Court action for alleged tendering cartel at WA mining camps
records.category: Competition and Exemptions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The ACCC has launched Federal Court proceedings against technology company Swift Networks Pty Ltd (Swift) for alleged bid rigging and price fixing when tendering to supply equipment and services to five Pilbara mining village sites.

records.title: Interim authorisation granted to Odin joint venture for marketing gas
records.summary: The ACCC has granted interim authorisation for Vintage Energy Ltd (ASX: VEN), Metgasco Ltd (ASX: MEL) and Bridgeport (Cooper Basin) Pty Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of New Hope Corporation Limited (ASX: NHC), to enter into joint gas marketing arrangements.

records.title: ACCC launches inquiry into deposit interest rates
records.summary: The ACCC will investigate how banks set interest rates for savers, including differences in interest rate increases between bank deposits and home loans after the Federal Government directed the ACCC to commence an inquiry into Australia’s retail deposit market.

records.title: Have a heart-to-heart with loved ones to help stop scams this Valentine's Day
records.summary: Australians are being urged to have a heart-to-heart with their friends and family members this Valentine’s Day, as they can play a vital role in protecting their loved ones from the heartache caused by scams.

records.title: ACCC and Italy's Competition Authority strengthen cooperation with new MOU
records.summary: Australia and Italy’s consumer and competition authorities are set to enhance cooperation and collaboration as the ACCC and the Autorità Garante della Concorrenza e del Mercato (AGCM) sign a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) today.

records.title: Employsure to pay $3m penalty for misleading Google ads after ACCC appeal
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement, Digital Platforms and Services
records.summary: The Full Federal Court has upheld the ACCC’s penalty appeal in a case against workplace relations advisor Employsure Pty Ltd and ordered Employsure to pay a penalty of $3 million for making false and misleading representations in its online ads that it was, or was affiliated with, a government agency.

records.title: TAFES can collectively bargain with Standards Australia, SAI Global and Techstreet
records.summary: The ACCC has not objected to the notification lodged by The National Copyright Unit (NCU) on behalf of the Copyright Advisory Group for TAFEs (CAG TAFE) and its members, Victorian TAFEs and other TAFE entities who may elect to join this group in the future (together, the Notifying Parties) on 15 November 2022 to collectively negotiate licence agreements for access to standards in TAFEs with Standards Australia Limited, SAI Global Australia Pty Ltd and/or Techstreet LLC.

records.title: ACCC social media sweep targets influencers
records.category: Advertising and Promotions, Compliance and Enforcement
records.summary: The ACCC has this week started a sweep to identify misleading testimonials and endorsements by social media influencers.

records.title: Gas supply outlook improves but shortfall still possible
records.summary: The ACCC’s latest gas inquiry report forecasts a supply shortfall of 30 petajoules (PJ) in Australia’s east coast gas market this year if the LNG producers were to export all their uncontracted gas.

records.title: ACCC consults on Sika's proposed divestiture undertaking
records.summary: The ACCC is seeking views on a proposed court-enforceable divestiture undertaking offered by Sika in relation to its proposed acquisition of MBCC Group.

records.title: Interim guidelines for gas industry after temporary price cap comes into force
records.summary: The ACCC has today published interim compliance and enforcement guidelines for the gas industry on the new Gas Market Emergency Price Order and explaining how the ACCC intends to exercise its enforcement role.

records.title: Consultation recommences on future regulation of the NBN
records.summary: The ACCC has today published a consultation paper on NBN Co’s proposed variation to its Special Access Undertaking and is inviting submissions from interested parties.

records.title: New year, new job? Beware of scammers
records.summary: Jobseekers are being urged to watch out for scammers, with new Scamwatch figures revealing Australians lost over $8.7 million to recruitment scams in 2022.
